papers_wanted.title: Psychology and Anomalous Observations: The Question of ESP in Dreams
papers_wanted.why_needed: Mainstream APA journal review of Maimonides dream-ESP studies, cited by Bem & Honorton (1994) as one of the first mainstream treatments. Important for establishing the pre-ganzfeld experimental tradition.

papers_wanted.title: Extraversion and ESP Performance: A Meta-Analysis and a New Confirmation
papers_wanted.why_needed: Meta-analysis of 60 studies (N~3000) on extraversion-psi correlation (r=.20, z=4.82 for free-response studies). Cited by Bem & Honorton 1994 as evidence for psychologically meaningful correlates of psi performance. Connects Honorton/Ferrari precognition meta-analysis program to ganzfeld individual differences.

papers_wanted.title: Human Performance Research: An Overview
papers_wanted.why_needed: NRC-commissioned comparative methodological analysis by Rosenthal (pioneer of meta-analysis). Found ganzfeld ESP studies were the ONLY area meeting basic sound experimental design requirements among 5 controversial areas. NRC chair asked Rosenthal to delete the parapsychology section; he refused. Central to the NRC controversy narrative.

papers_wanted.title: Reply to the National Research Council Study on Parapsychology
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Extended refutation of the 1988 NRC report's negative conclusions on parapsychology. Protests the committee's treatment of ganzfeld evidence and its failure to acknowledge the Hyman-Honorton joint communique. Important for the NRC controversy and meta-debate.

papers_wanted.title: Quantum Processes Predicted?
papers_wanted.why_needed: First published RNG-PK experiment. Schmidt pioneered the use of electronic random number generators for psychokinesis testing, initiating the entire research program that the Bosch et al. meta-analysis covers. Foundational to PK paradigm.

papers_wanted.title: A Ganzfeld Study Using Identical Twins
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Precursor to Parker/Jensen twin physiological connectedness program (jensen_2012_entangled, parker_2013_further). Uses ganzfeld paradigm with identical twins. Part of the same research program; cited by parker_2013_further. Would fill a gap in the twin telepathy section.
sources_not_yet_tried: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research website

papers_wanted.title: Effects of Intentionally Treated Water on the Growth of Mesenchymal Stem Cells: An Exploratory Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Most direct precursor to yu_2024_effects: same paradigm (intentionally treated water + cell biology) by same research group. Demonstrated treated water enhanced proliferation and gene expression of human mesenchymal stem cells. Key link in the Shiah/Radin treated-water series.

papers_wanted.title: Effects of Intentionally Treated Water on Growth of Arabidopsis thaliana Seeds with Cryptochrome Mutations
papers_wanted.why_needed: Earlier study in the Shiah/Radin treated-water series demonstrating intention effects on Arabidopsis plants, especially cryptochrome gain-of-function mutants. Provides the basis for the cryptochrome mechanism theory proposed in yu_2024_effects. Cited by multiple later papers in the series.

papers_wanted.title: Psi-Mediated Imagery and Ideation in an Experimental Procedure for Regulating Perceptual Input
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Founding paper of the Ganzfeld paradigm for psi research — the single most influential experimental method in parapsychology. Cited by Moulton & Kosslyn 2008, Bem & Honorton 1994, and referenced throughout the ganzfeld literature.

papers_wanted.title: Event-Related Potential (ERP) Indicators of Unconscious Psi: A Replication Using Subjects Unselected for Psi
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Moulton & Kosslyn 2008 fMRI study — the ERP guessing-task paradigm that Moulton adapted for neuroimaging. Part of the Warren/Don/McDonough ERP-psi series that found differential brain responses to psi targets.

papers_wanted.title: An Evaluation of Remote Viewing: Research and Applications
papers_wanted.why_needed: The AIR evaluation of the Stargate remote viewing program — the government-commissioned review that led to program termination. Foundational reference for remote viewing debate. Cited by Moulton 2008 and relevant to Controversy #10.

papers_wanted.title: Testing Alleged Mediumship: Methods and Results
papers_wanted.why_needed: Skeptical mediumship study — null result using controlled methods. Provides the skeptical counterpoint to Beischel's positive AIRII results (2015). Would improve balance in Controversy #6 (Mediumship).
sources_not_yet_tried: British Journal of Psychology via Wiley

papers_wanted.title: A Controlled Long-Distance Test of a Professional Medium
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent controlled test of mediumship accuracy (n=7, not significant). One of the few mediumship studies outside the Windbridge group. Cited by Beischel 2015 as comparison.

papers_wanted.title: Mindsight: Near-Death and Out-of-Body Experiences in the Blind
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational study of NDEs in congenitally blind persons who report visual experiences during NDEs despite never having experienced vision. Provides one of the strongest challenges to the REM-intrusion model (Long & Holden 2007 cite this as evidence that REM cannot explain NDEs in blind people without rapid eye movements). Cited by long_2007_arousal_nde_response.

papers_wanted.title: Light and Death: One Doctor's Fascinating Account of Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Contains the detailed documented case of Pam Reynolds, who experienced a comprehensive NDE during brain surgery while under full general anesthesia with EEG monitoring, taped eyes, and clicking ear inserts. Her accurate veridical perceptions during the surgery are among the most cited evidence against reductionist NDE explanations. Referenced extensively in long_2007_arousal_nde_response.
sources_not_yet_tried: Book — may not be available as PDF

papers_wanted.title: Dying to Live: Science and the Near-Death Experience
papers_wanted.why_needed: The most influential skeptical book on NDEs, proposing physiological mechanisms for each NDE feature (including the visual cortex model for tunnel/light effects cited in Mobbs & Watt 2011). A foundational skeptical text for Controversy #7. Cited by mobbs_2011_nothing_paranormal.

papers_wanted.title: On the Quantum Mechanics of Consciousness, with Application to Anomalous Phenomena
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational PEAR theoretical paper proposing quantum wave-function model of consciousness. Underwent 15 peer reviews before publication. Cited extensively in jahn_2005_pear (Ref 10) and central to PEAR theoretical framework. Fills theory gap in folder 09.

papers_wanted.title: Wellness Implications of Retroactive Influence: Exploring an Outrageous Hypothesis
papers_wanted.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key theoretical reference on wellness/health implications of retrocausal influence, directly cited by Radin (2007) as inspiration for retrocausal placebo idea. Bridges presentiment research with healing paradigm.

papers_wanted.title: Dying to Know the Truth: Visions of a Dying Brain, or False Memories?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key theoretical counterpoint arguing NDE reports may be false memories. Referenced by Thonnard et al. (2013) as the specific hypothesis their MCQ study was designed to test. Provides skeptical NDE perspective from a high-impact journal.

papers_wanted.title: Performance at a Precognitive Remote Viewing Task, With and Without Ganzfeld Stimulation: Three Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Three contemporary RV experiments combining remote viewing with ganzfeld, cited multiple times by Escola-Gascon et al. (2023). Provides fresh evidence for RV with modern methodology from an independent group.

papers_wanted.title: Seven Experiments That Could Change The World
papers_wanted.why_needed: The book that inspired the entire psychic pet research program, directly motivating both the Wiseman and Sheldrake Jaytee experiments. Primary source for understanding the origin of this research line.

papers_wanted.title: Parapsychology: A Handbook for the 21st Century
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive reference handbook that Wahbeh et al. 2018 cites as foundational background on paranormal belief measurement and EHE research history. Would strengthen the methodology/overview section of the library.

papers_wanted.title: Facilitated Communication Since 1995: A Review of Published Studies
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Autism and Developmental Disorders
papers_wanted.why_needed: Chen et al. 2012 explicitly mentions Facilitated Communication (FC) as the negative comparison case that RPM is designed to avoid. Mostert 2001 is the standard FC debunking review; having it in the library would anchor the skeptical side of the autism/nonverbal controversy (#9).

papers_wanted.title: Yogic Attainment in Relation to Awareness of Precognitive Targets
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Alibalaei 2025. First study finding evidence for psi in advanced Siddha Yoga meditators but not novices at an Indian ashram. Key test of the yoga-psi link.

papers_wanted.title: The Potential Impact of Yoga Practices on Extraordinary Human Abilities: A Scoping Review
papers_wanted.why_needed: Scoping review of yoga-psi literature by the same first author as Alibalaei 2025. Maps the theoretical background for the experimental study.
sources_not_yet_tried: Journal of Applied Consciousness Studies website

papers_wanted.title: Implicit Intuition: How Heart Rate Can Contribute to Prediction of Future Events
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Tressoldi 2011 pupil dilation study — used heart rate to predict alerting vs. neutral sounds with 56% accuracy. First in the Tressoldi prediction-accuracy research line that the 2011 study extends.

papers_wanted.title: The Ganzfeld Psi Experiments: A Critical Appraisal
papers_wanted.why_needed: The foundational skeptical critique of the original ganzfeld studies that prompted the Hyman-Honorton joint communique and the autoganzfeld reforms. Cited by Alcock (2011) and essential to Controversy 1 (ganzfeld replication). The library has Hyman 1994 and 2010 but not this earlier, more influential critique.

papers_wanted.title: Meta-analytic procedures and the nature of replication: The ganzfeld debate
papers_wanted.reason: Foundational early ganzfeld meta-analysis (28 studies, z=6.60, p=4.1e-11). Historically important predecessor to Bem & Honorton 1994 autoganzfeld meta. Cited in Schooler et al. 2018.

papers_wanted.title: A Quantum Mechanical Random Number Generator for Psi Tests
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper introducing the first electronic RNG for psi testing, based on radioactive decay. All subsequent RNG/micro-PK experiments descend from this design. Cited by Kauffman & Radin (2023) and underlies the entire PK/RNG evidence base reviewed in multiple library meta-analyses.

papers_wanted.title: Positive therapeutic effects of intercessory prayer in a coronary care unit population
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational prayer-healing RCT (N=393 CCU patients), one of the earliest controlled prayer studies. Cited by Schwartz & Dossey 2010 and central to the healing-intention debate.

papers_wanted.title: A randomized double-blind study of the effect of distant healing in a population with advanced AIDS
papers_wanted.why_needed: Distant healing RCT in advanced AIDS patients. Key positive healing study cited by Schwartz & Dossey 2010. Elisabeth Targ's landmark study.

papers_wanted.title: Commentary: a method used to train skeptical volunteers to heal in an experimental setting
papers_wanted.why_needed: Describes the Bengston rapid-imaging healing technique used by skeptical volunteers. Key method paper for the Bengston healing approach. Published in JACM.

papers_wanted.title: Doing Bayesian Data Analysis: A Tutorial with R and BUGS
papers_wanted.why_needed: Standard Bayesian analysis textbook cited by Kennedy 2014 for discussion of power in Bayesian context. Foundation reference for Bayesian methods in psi research.

papers_wanted.title: PK Effect on Pre-Recorded Targets
papers_wanted.why_needed: First published report of PK effects on pre-recorded random events. Foundational for Schmidt (1987) theoretical framework and all subsequent time-displaced PK research. Published in JASPR.

papers_wanted.title: The Quantum Theory of Psi Phenomena
papers_wanted.why_needed: Influential theoretical paper proposing quantum mechanical model for psi, cited by Schmidt (1987). Key early quantum-consciousness theory alongside Mattuck & Walker (1979). Published in Psychoenergetic Systems.

papers_wanted.title: A Rasch Scaling Validation of a Core Near-Death Experience
papers_wanted.why_needed: Rasch analysis establishing the NDE Scale as a unidimensional interval measure. Key psychometric validation cited by Greyson (2007). Published in Br J Psychol.

papers_wanted.title: Brain-to-Brain (Mind-to-Mind) Interaction at Distance: A Confirmatory Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: The actual Tressoldi 2014 EEG correlations paper (F1000Research) — the catalog had its authors confused with the 2011 behavioral study. These 8 authors belong to this paper, not the ideogram study.

papers_wanted.title: Method for measuring the probability of 1 bit extrasensory information transfer between living organisms
papers_wanted.why_needed: Pioneered the proportional SCR analysis method used by Spottiswoode & May (2003). Foundational for the presentiment/physiological psi detection paradigm. Published in Journal of Parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: Features of 'near-death experience' in relation to whether or not patients were near death
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key precursor to Charland-Verville et al. (2014): compared NDE phenomenology between patients who were vs were not actually near death, finding comparable experiences. Published in The Lancet.

papers_wanted.title: An attempt to reproduce the presentiment EDA response
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent Japanese replication attempt of presentiment EDA response. Cited by Spottiswoode & May (2003). Published in Journal of International Society of Life Information Science.

papers_wanted.title: Entangled Minds: Extrasensory Experiences in a Quantum Reality
papers_wanted.why_needed: Radin's major popular-science book presenting theoretical framework for psi effects via quantum entanglement. Cited as theoretical basis in Radin 2008 water crystal study. Would fill gap in folder 12 (Books and Overviews).

papers_wanted.title: The Hidden Messages in Water
papers_wanted.why_needed: The source claim that Radin 2006/2008 triple-blind studies attempted to test scientifically. Understanding the original claim is important for contextualizing the experimental tests.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness-related anomalies in double-slit experiments: Comment on a false positive claim
papers_wanted.why_needed: Directly critiqued the unpublished Radin 2012-2013 double-slit experiment (now radin_2021_psychophysical), claiming a control condition was a false positive. Radin et al. responded that Walleczek failed to adjust for multiple comparisons. Essential for the double-slit controversy.

papers_wanted.title: Physics and claims for anomalous effects related to consciousness
papers_wanted.why_needed: Published in the same JCS special issue as Alcock 2003. Reports null results from double-slit consciousness experiments at York University — directly relevant to Radin's double-slit program. Alcock cites Jeffers' null results as evidence against psi.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness-related interactions in a double-slit optical system
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent (non-IONS) replication attempt of Radin's double-slit paradigm. One of only 4 labs to have conducted this experiment. Important for assessing the replicability of the double-slit consciousness effect.

papers_wanted.title: Some biological effects of the laying-on of hands: A review of experiments with animals and plants
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_wanted: Foundational healing-intention study cited by Schwartz 2015 as precursor to water IR spectroscopy research. Demonstrated biological effects of laying-on-of-hands on wound healing in mice and plant growth. One of the earliest controlled experiments in healing intention.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness Induced Restoration of Time Symmetry (CIRTS): A Psychophysical Theoretical Perspective
papers_wanted.why_wanted: Theoretical framework (CIRTS) for presentiment effects cited by Radin 2011 and relevant to the entire presentiment research line. Proposes consciousness-induced time symmetry as mechanism for retrocausal effects.

papers_wanted.title: Can a slide-show presentiment effect be discovered in brain electrical activity?
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_wanted: EEG presentiment study using slow cortical potentials cited by Radin 2011. Would add an independent lab's perspective on EEG-based presentiment to complement the IONS program.

papers_wanted.title: A Randomized, Controlled Trial of the Effects of Remote, Intercessory Prayer on Outcomes in Patients Admitted to the Coronary Care Unit
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of the two foundational prayer RCTs cited by Leibovici (2001) as motivation; the other major prayer study alongside Byrd (1988). Showed beneficial effects of remote intercessory prayer in CCU patients. Essential context for the distant healing controversy (#5).

papers_wanted.title: Autonomic Detection of Remote Observation: Two Conceptual Replications
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the Parapsychological Association 37th Annual Convention
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Wiseman & Schlitz (1997): Schlitz's positive staring results from this study prompted Wiseman's null replication attempts, leading to their joint collaboration. Essential for understanding the experimenter-effect series.

papers_wanted.title: Evidence of anomalous information retrieval between two research mediums: Replication and extension
papers_wanted.why_needed: Pioneering study on mediumship accuracy under controlled conditions. Early replication of anomalous information retrieval by mediums. Cited by delorme_2013_electrocortical; a foundational precursor to the IONS/Windbridge mediumship program.

papers_wanted.title: Quantitative analysis of research mediums' conscious experiences during a discarnate reading versus a control task: A pilot study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Quantitative phenomenological study of mediums' conscious experiences during readings. Complements EEG/neuroimaging studies (delorme_2013, peres_2012) by characterizing the subjective side of mediumistic states. Published in Australian Journal of Parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: A Meta-Analysis of ESP Studies Contrasting Hypnosis and a Comparison Condition
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of the 11 meta-analyses included in Tressoldi & Storm (2021) umbrella review; the only meta-analysis examining hypnosis as a moderator of ESP performance; found hypnosis condition yielded proportion index .524 vs .505 for normal state, directly relevant to Tressoldi's moderator analysis showing altered states enhance anomalous cognition

papers_wanted.title: A Qualitative and Quantitative Study of the Incidence, Features and Aetiology of Near Death Experience in Cardiac Arrest Survivors
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of the four foundational prospective NDE studies (alongside van Lommel 2001, Greyson 2003, Sartori 2006). Cited by van Lommel 2013, Greyson 2014, and multiple other library papers. The British arm of the prospective NDE research that established 11% NDE incidence in cardiac arrest survivors. Precursor to the larger AWARE study (parnia_2014_aware).

papers_wanted.title: The Decline Effect in Spontaneous and Experimental Psychical Research
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive review of decline effects specific to parapsychology, cited by Radin 2008; would fill gap in methodology/theory section.

papers_wanted.title: Memorandum for the Record, re: Analysis of the Global Consciousness Project’s Data near the 11 September 2001 Events
papers_wanted.why_needed: Critical/independent assessment of the GCP Sept 11 data; confirmed statistics but questioned timing — would provide balance to Nelson 2002.

papers_wanted.title: AWAreness during REsuscitation - II: A Multi-Center Study of Consciousness and Awareness in Cardiac Arrest
papers_wanted.why_needed: Largest prospective study of consciousness during cardiac arrest (AWARE-II); distinguishes NDEs from dream-like/delusional experiences via structured interviews; cited by Martial et al. 2025; successor to AWARE-I (parnia_2014_aware) already in library; would strengthen Controversy 7 (NDE evidence)

papers_wanted.title: A Systematic Analysis of Distressing Near-Death Experience Accounts
papers_wanted.why_needed: Only systematic analysis of distressing NDEs (at least 14% of cases); fills gap in Controversy 7 by documenting negative NDE valence; from the same Coma Science Group as martial_2017_nde_features and martial_2025_nde_clinical_relevance; critical for clinical NDE management recommendations

papers_wanted.title: Precognitive Dreaming: Investigating Anomalous Cognition and Psychological Factors
papers_wanted.why_needed: The largest post-Maimonides dream ESP study (200 trials, 32% hit rate, p = .015), pre-registered, file-drawer free, from a skeptical experimenter at the KPU. Central to the Baptista et al. (2014) chapter. Would strengthen the dream ESP evidence base in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Viewing the Future: A Pilot Study with an Error-Detecting Protocol
papers_wanted.why_needed: Replicated ARV silver futures experiment with improved error-detection protocol: 11/12 correct predictions (p = 0.003). Direct precursor to modern ARV financial applications. Cited by Lee (2008) as key evidence for applied RV.

papers_wanted.title: A Bias Caused by Inappropriate Averaging in Experiments with Randomized Stimuli
papers_wanted.why_needed: Identifies a specific statistical artifact in presentiment experiments where inappropriate averaging of randomized stimulus sequences can produce false positive effects. Key methodological reference cited by both Mossbridge et al. (2015) and Kennedy (2013) in the presentiment debate.

papers_wanted.title: Neural Correlates of the Psychedelic State as Determined by fMRI Studies with Psilocybin
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational evidence for DMN deactivation during psilocybin experience, directly cited by Woollacott 2020 filter theory. Key to understanding neural mechanisms proposed in Controversy #7 (NDE consciousness survival). One of the most cited neuroimaging papers in psychedelic research.

papers_wanted.title: Meditation Experience Is Associated with Differences in Default Mode Network Activity and Connectivity
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key evidence that meditation deactivates DMN nodes, directly cited by Woollacott 2020 as primary neuroimaging support for meditation-psilocybin-NDE convergence. Relevant to filter theory debate in Controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Classic Hallucinogens and Mystical Experiences: Phenomenology and Neural Correlates
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive review linking classic hallucinogens to mystical experience phenomenology and DMN deactivation — the most-cited reference in Woollacott 2020. Key to the psychedelic-NDE-meditation convergence argument in Controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Physiological Correlates of Psi Cognition
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational study measuring EDA, blood volume, and heart rate during agent-receiver psi tasks—the direct experimental precursor to Radin's 1997 presentiment paradigm. Cited in radin_1997_unconscious_presentiment as the earliest example of physiological psi detection.

papers_wanted.title: Bayesian Analysis of Random Event Generator Data
papers_wanted.why_needed: Bayesian critique of RNG/PK analysis methodology, cited by Scargle (2002) as 'absolutely on-point' for GCP statistical methods. Would strengthen the skeptical/methodological side of Controversy #8 (GCP).

papers_wanted.title: A Bayesian Maximum-Entropy Approach to Hypothesis Testing, for Application to RNG and Similar Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Bayesian methodology paper for RNG experiments, cited by Scargle (2002) alongside Jefferys (1990) as essential reading for proper GCP statistical analysis. Would fill the methodology gap in Controversy #8.

papers_wanted.title: Surge of Neurophysiological Coherence and Connectivity in the Dying Brain
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational neuroscience paper showing gamma-band surge in dying rat brains. Cited by Timmermann 2018 and both sides of the NDE debate. Key evidence for neurobiological NDE mechanisms. Reviewer for Timmermann's study.

papers_wanted.title: N,N-Dimethyltryptamine and the Pineal Gland: Separating Fact from Myth
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major critique of the endogenous DMT hypothesis for NDEs. Cited by Timmermann 2018 as the counter-argument. Would fill a skeptical gap in the NDE neurobiological debate.

papers_wanted.title: Comparison of Brazilian Spiritist Mediumship and Dissociative Identity Disorder
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key study distinguishing healthy mediumistic dissociation from pathological DID; directly relevant to Controversy #6 (mediumship) and the differential diagnosis question. Cited by Peres 2012 for mental health screening methodology. Co-authored by Moreira-Almeida (Peres co-author) and Cardeña.

papers_wanted.title: Neural Correlates of a Mystical Experience in Carmelite Nuns
papers_wanted.why_needed: Neuroimaging study of mystical religious experience in Carmelite nuns; companion to Peres 2012 as another neuroscience study of spiritual/religious altered states. Relevant to the broader question of neural correlates of anomalous experiences.

papers_wanted.title: "Positive" Results Increase Down the Hierarchy of the Sciences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Empirical demonstration that positive-result rates increase systematically from physics (70%) down through biology to psychology (~90%), providing quantitative evidence for the publication-bias gradient that underlies critiques of psi evidence. Cited by LeBel & Peters (2011) in their analysis of the file-drawer problem. Directly relevant to Controversy #10 (meta-debate) and any discussion of how psychology's positive-result bias interacts with psi claims.
sources_not_yet_tried: PLoS ONE (open access — should be freely available)

papers_wanted.title: How Many Scientists Fabricate and Falsify Research? A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Survey Data
papers_wanted.why_needed: Directly cited by John et al. (2012) as the prior meta-analytic estimate of QRP prevalence (33.7% upper bound) that the BTS study surpassed. Provides the baseline for understanding the replication crisis and is essential context for the meta-debate about psi research quality.

papers_wanted.title: Scientists Behaving Badly
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational survey of scientific misconduct among NIH-funded scientists (N=3,247): 33% admitted at least one of 10 questionable behaviors in the past three years. Directly cited in John et al. (2012) and Simmons et al. (2011). Would complete the QRP trilogy in folder 10 alongside John 2012 and Simmons 2011.

papers_wanted.title: A methodology for the objective study of transpersonal imagery
papers_wanted.why_needed: Primary data paper underlying the 15-experiment DMILS series summarized in braud_1993_remote_mental_influence_eda. Contains session-level statistics, full experimental rationales, and methodological details not presented in the 1993 summary. Essential complement to both braud_1993 and braud_schlitz_1991_consciousness_interactions. High-quality long-form empirical paper (~50 pages) in JSE.

papers_wanted.title: 'I'm not afraid to die': The loss of the fear of death after a near-death experience
papers_wanted.why_needed: Theoretical paper proposing that four specific NDE features (disembodiment, positive emotions, bright light, spiritual beings) reduce fear of death. Its predictions are empirically tested and partially refuted by greyson_2022_nde_fear_death, which finds disembodiment does NOT predict fear reduction. Needed to complete the theory-test pairing.

papers_wanted.title: Theoretical risks and tabular asterisks: Sir Karl, Sir Ronald, and the slow progress of soft psychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Classic critique of NHST in 'soft psychology': Meehl's conjecture (in nonexperimental research with large N, roughly 50% of directional predictions are confirmed by chance) is a central empirical claim in Gigerenzer's (2004) critique. Foundational for any serious methodology discussion in parapsychology; Kennedy's methodology series cites this tradition.

papers_wanted.title: Do studies of statistical power have an effect on the power of studies?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Gigerenzer's (2004) own 1989 power audit found that average statistical power in psychology for medium effects had actually decreased over the 24 years since Cohen's (1962) original audit. This empirical finding is cited to support the claim that low-power critiques of psi are just as applicable to mainstream psychology. Fills a gap in the methodology chain.

papers_wanted.title: Terminal lucidity: A review and a case collection
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key review of terminal lucidity (unexpected mental clarity before death in dementia/brain-damage patients). Cited prominently in delorme_2021_survival_consciousness as indirect evidence that mind and brain are separable. Would fill a significant gap in folder 07's coverage of survival-adjacent anomalies.

papers_wanted.title: Rating the Persuasiveness of Empirical Evidence for the Survival of Consciousness After Bodily Death: A cross-sectional study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct companion to delorme_2021_survival_consciousness: reports the full academic survey (N=422) on which survival experiments would be most persuasive to scientists. Published in Journal of Anomalous Experience and Cognition (JAEX). Fills the gap between the essay and its empirical survey component.

papers_wanted.title: Spontaneous remission of dementia before death: Results from a study on terminal lucidity
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited in delorme_2021_survival_consciousness as showing >80% of terminal lucidity cases involve complete return of memory and orientation; key empirical support for the claim that mind may not be fully reducible to brain activity. Prospective component strengthens the case over purely retrospective studies.

papers_wanted.title: Transparent Psi Project: A Pre-Registered Replication of Bem's (2011) 'Retroactive Facilitation of Recall' Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Directly cited in Kennedy 2024: Kennedy conducted a formal research audit of this study (finding a significant software error) and uses it as a model of how FDA-style quality control applies in academic psi research. A pre-registered, high-quality Bem replication attempt — critical for replication chain of bem_2011_feeling.

papers_wanted.title: The Influence of an Unorthodox Method of Treatment on Wound Healing in Mice
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational animal healing study cited explicitly in Bengston & Krinsley (2000) as the pioneer for animal healing research. Grad studied Hungarian healer Oskar Estebany's ability to accelerate wound healing in mice — the methodological template Bengston extended to cancer.

papers_wanted.title: Scientific Misconduct and the Myth of Self-Correction in Science
papers_wanted.why_needed: Extensively cited throughout Kennedy 2024 as the key post-Stapel analysis of research misconduct. Provides the sociological/psychological analysis of why fraud is common and why self-correction in science fails — foundational for evaluating data integrity standards in parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: Psychic Pets: A Survey in North-West England
papers_wanted.why_needed: Precursor survey to the Jaytee experimental program; establishes that 46% of dog owners in NW England noticed anticipatory return behavior and that 16% reported reactions >10 min in advance. Foundational prevalence data for the animal-telepathy research program.
papers_wanted.notes: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research, 68, 353-364. Cited by sheldrake_smart_1998_dog_preliminary.

papers_wanted.title: Handedness and Motor Skills in Autism Spectrum Disorder: A Meta-Analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key meta-analysis (cited in donnellan_2013_rethinking) confirming widespread alterations in motor performance across ASD studies since 1981; concludes motor deficits are a potential core feature of ASD. Needed for Folder 11 evidence base.

papers_wanted.title: Intensive Insight Meditation: A Phenomenological Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Pioneering mixed-methods study of American Buddhist meditators during 3-month Vipassana retreat; cited by Lindahl et al. (2017) as foundational work that yielded reports of unusual experiences including strong negative emotions, involuntary movements, anomalous somatic sensations, and out-of-body experiences. Historical precursor to VCE study.

papers_wanted.title: Adverse Effects of Meditation: A Preliminary Investigation of Long-Term Meditators
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of only prospective studies to deliberately query adverse effects; found 63% of meditators on intensive Vipassana retreat reported at least one adverse effect, 7.4% stopped meditating due to negative effects. Cited by Lindahl as key precedent for systematic investigation of meditation challenges.

papers_wanted.title: A Qualitative Summary of Experiential Challenges Associated with Meditation Practice
papers_wanted.why_needed: Qualitative study of active meditators that received both positive and negative reports including exacerbation of psychological problems, troubling experiences of self, and reality being challenged (including OBEs and one hospitalization for psychosis). Cited by Lindahl as important recent precedent, though limited by all-male sample from one community.

papers_wanted.title: Starlight, Starbright: Are Stars Conscious?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Central to Sheldrake 2021 - Matloff's volitional star hypothesis proposes stars control their galactic positions via electromagnetic jets. Sheldrake builds his solar consciousness argument partly on this hypothesis. Foundational for stellar consciousness literature.

papers_wanted.title: The conscious electromagnetic information (Cemi) field theory: The hard problem made easy?
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of the eight electromagnetic field theories of consciousness discussed by Sheldrake. CEMI theory proposes brain's EM field is the physical substrate of consciousness. Sheldrake applies this to argue solar EM fields could be conscious.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness: Here, there and everywhere?
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the Royal Society B
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key IIT paper cited by Sheldrake. Integrated Information Theory is one of the main frameworks Sheldrake uses to evaluate solar consciousness. Essential for understanding the panpsychist turn in consciousness studies.

papers_wanted.title: Galileo's Error: Foundations for a New Science of Consciousness
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major contemporary panpsychist work cited by Sheldrake. Goff argues for panpsychism as solution to the hard problem of consciousness. Represents the philosophical foundation for extending consciousness to non-biological systems.

papers_wanted.title: Solving the 'hard problem': Consciousness as an intrinsic property of magnetic fields
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Consciousness Exploration and Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of the electromagnetic field theories Sheldrake discusses. Murphy proposes magnetic fields (not electrical) are the primary substrate of consciousness. Sheldrake notes this theory's relevance to solar consciousness given the sun's magnetic field complexity.

papers_wanted.title: One Mind: How Our Individual Mind Is Part of a Greater Consciousness and Why It Matters
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Sheldrake (2019) as contrasting 'one mind' approach to psi; important theoretical framework for consciousness survival debates and NDE research. Provides alternative to morphic field model.

papers_wanted.title: Wholeness and the Implicate Order
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational physics/philosophy work cited by Sheldrake as possible mechanism for morphic resonance via implicate order. Essential for understanding theoretical foundations of non-local consciousness models.

papers_wanted.title: Perspectives of Clinical Parapsychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational guide for clinicians working with anomalous experiences. Cited by Rabeyron (2022) as key reference for clinical parapsychology approach. Result of 2007 international experts meeting on anomalous experiences. Fills gap in clinical methodology literature.

papers_wanted.title: Varieties of Anomalous Experience: Examining the Scientific Evidence (2nd ed.)
papers_wanted.why_needed: APA-published foundational handbook on anomalous experiences. Cited extensively by Rabeyron (2022) for definitions and phenomenological classification. Essential reference work for the field. Already have cardena_2014_handbook which appears to be different paper.

papers_wanted.title: Abduction: Human Encounters with Aliens
papers_wanted.why_needed: Source of 'ontological shock' concept used throughout Rabeyron (2022). Foundational work on alien abduction experiences from Harvard psychiatrist. Important for understanding trauma aspects of anomalous experiences and clinical response.

papers_wanted.title: Agent-percipient relationship and GESP performance
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Sheldrake 2002 as evidence that emotionally close people communicate telepathically better than strangers. Foundational for emotional closeness hypothesis in telepathy research.

papers_wanted.title: GESP experiments with the free-response method
papers_wanted.why_needed: Historical precursor cited by Sheldrake 2002. Early free-response telepathy methodology that informed later ganzfeld research. Important for understanding methodological development.

papers_wanted.title: Autoganzfeld II: An attempted replication of the PRL ganzfeld research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Sheldrake 2002 for finding exceptionally high hit rates in parent-child and sibling pairs. Direct evidence for emotional closeness facilitating telepathy in controlled experiments.

papers_wanted.title: Who's Calling? Evaluating the Accuracy of Guessing Who Is on the Phone
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent U.S. automated two-caller replication explicitly analyzed in the 2025 telecommunication telepathy meta-analysis; needed to strengthen the independent-replication branch of Controversy 13.

papers_wanted.title: A Comparison of Four New Automated Tests for Telephone Telepathy
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Anomalous Experience and Cognition
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct methodological precursor to the 2025 meta-analysis; includes four automated protocol variants and clarifies which implementation choices produce null vs positive outcomes.
sources_not_yet_tried: Journal of Anomalous Experience and Cognition archive

papers_wanted.title: Decoherence and the Quantum-to-Classical Transition
papers_wanted.why_needed: Definitive work on decoherence theory cited extensively by Kennedy; essential for understanding quantum-to-classical transition arguments

papers_wanted.title: Information Theory, Evolution, and the Origin of Life
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational work on information theory applied to origin of life; Kennedy cites for genetic code information arguments

papers_wanted.title: Mind, Matter and Quantum Mechanics (3rd ed.)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major consciousness-quantum theory work; Kennedy critiques Stapp's 'idealike' quantum domain characterization

papers_wanted.title: Perceptive Pets: A Survey in Northwest California
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Previous survey by the same authors in the same geographic area (Santa Cruz County). This 2001 paper references it for methodology and for the finding that pet owners reported more psychic experiences. Important for understanding the research program's development.

papers_wanted.title: Representative National Surveys of Psychic Phenomena
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major review of national surveys on psychic phenomena cited in this paper (58% US rate for telepathic experiences). Provides important comparative context for prevalence rates. Foundational reference for survey methodology in parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: Mind and Cosmos: Why the Materialist Neo-Darwinian Conception of Nature Is Almost Certainly False
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major philosophical critique of materialist neo-Darwinism by prominent secular philosopher. Cited by Walach 2020 regarding consciousness unexplained by materialism. Important for Controversy 10 (meta-debate) and provides external philosophical support for non-materialist ontology from outside parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: Prestimulus response to emotional stimuli: A systematic review with a meta-analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark presentiment meta-analysis of 26 studies showing physiological responses 1-10s before unpredictable stimuli (fixed effect d=0.21, p<2.71x10^-12). Foundational evidence for precognition cited in Wahbeh & Radin 2022 and dozens of other papers.

papers_wanted.title: A Meta-Analysis of Ganzfeld ESP Studies with Physical and Dynamic Targets
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive ganzfeld meta-analysis of 29 studies (1,471 trials) showing 31.3% hit rate vs 25% chance (odds against chance > 10^18 to 1). Most important modern ganzfeld analysis. Cited in Wahbeh & Radin 2022 as key evidence for telepathy.

papers_wanted.title: The Conscious Mind: In Search of a Fundamental Theory
papers_wanted.why_needed: Seminal philosophy book introducing the 'hard problem of consciousness' - explains why physicalist theories cannot account for qualia/subjective experience. Cited in Wahbeh & Radin 2022 as foundational to the consciousness debate. Essential theoretical reference.

papers_wanted.title: Integrated Information Theory: From Consciousness to Its Physical Substrate
papers_wanted.why_needed: Authoritative IIT overview explaining the axioms and postulates. Wahbeh & Radin 2022 discuss IIT as potentially bridgeable to non-local consciousness. Important physicalist theory for comparison.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness in the universe: A review of the 'Orch OR' theory
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive Orch-OR theory review proposing quantum consciousness via microtubules. Wahbeh & Radin 2022 discuss as a testable non-local consciousness mechanism. Penrose is Nobel laureate, theory is increasingly testable.

papers_wanted.title: Religious Priming: A Meta-Analysis With a Focus on Prosociality
papers_wanted.why_needed: Target of van Elk et al. (2015) critique - essential to have both sides of this methodological debate. van Elk et al. re-analyze this paper's data and reach opposite conclusions about religious priming effects.

papers_wanted.title: Meta-Regression Approximations to Reduce Publication Selection Bias
papers_wanted.why_needed: Introduces PET-PEESE method used in van Elk et al. (2015) re-analysis. Foundational methodology paper for bias correction in meta-analysis.

papers_wanted.title: A Bayesian Approach to Mitigation of Publication Bias
papers_wanted.why_needed: Introduces Bayesian bias correction method used in van Elk et al. (2015). One of the two competing bias-correction approaches compared in the paper.

papers_wanted.title: Self-ascribed paranormal ability: a reflexive thematic analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by the editorial as foundational research on self-ascribed paranormal abilities. The editorial explicitly references this paper's findings on how individuals integrate paranormal abilities into identity. Key empirical basis for the editorial's framework.

papers_wanted.title: Paranormal belief, conspiracy theory endorsement, and positive wellbeing: a network analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Part of the same Research Topic introduced by the editorial. Editorial highlights its key finding: paranormal beliefs link to adaptive coping while conspiracy theories link to avoidant coping. Important for understanding psychological functions of paranormal belief.

papers_wanted.title: Superstitious, magical, and paranormal beliefs: an integrative model
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical paper cited in editorial's introduction. Proposes integrative model linking paranormal beliefs to cognitive biases and analytic thinking styles. Frequently cited framework in paranormal belief research.

papers_wanted.title: Sensory processing sensitivity, transliminality, and boundary-thinness as predictors of anomalous experiences, beliefs, and abilities
papers_wanted.why_needed: Recent empirical paper cited by editorial. Examines cognitive-perceptual predictors (SPS, transliminality, boundary-thinness) of anomalous experiences. Connects to editorial's focus on individual differences in psi experiences.

papers_wanted.title: Future directions in precognition research: more research can bridge the gap between skeptics and proponents
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Duggan & Tressoldi (2018) as promising development using software to predict meaningful future outcomes in real-time. Important for understanding the evolution toward real-time prediction paradigms.

papers_wanted.title: Independent verification of psychophysical interactions with a double-slit interference pattern
papers_wanted.why_needed: Partial re-analysis cited by Tremblay that raised concerns about physical noise in the system. Important skeptical perspective that complements Tremblay's statistical critique.

papers_wanted.title: Dogs That Know When Their Owners Are Coming Home, and Other Unexplained Powers of Animals
papers_wanted.why_needed: Sheldrake's influential book on animal telepathy. Cited by Sheldrake & Smart (2003) as background for distance effects in telepathy. Important for understanding the broader research program connecting animal and human telepathy.

papers_wanted.title: The 'psychic pet' phenomenon: a reply to Rupert Sheldrake
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct reply to Sheldrake's reanalysis of the Wiseman et al. 1998 data. Important for understanding the methodological debate between skeptics and pro-psi researchers. Cited in Sheldrake & Smart 2000.
sources_not_yet_tried: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research archive

papers_wanted.title: ESP and Psychokinesis: A Philosophical Examination
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational philosophical work on ESP and psychokinesis cited by Sheldrake 2014. Provides philosophical framework for psi research that underpins the telepathy experimental program.

papers_wanted.title: The Mystery of Telepathy
papers_wanted.why_needed: Book on telepathy cited by Sheldrake 2014. Part of the historical literature on telepathy research that provides context for modern experimental work.

papers_wanted.title: The Sense of Being Stared At, and Other Aspects of the Extended Mind
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key book by Sheldrake introducing the sense of being stared at phenomenon and morphic resonance theory. Cited in 2014 telepathy overview. Provides theoretical framework connecting telepathy to broader extended mind concepts.

papers_wanted.title: The Elusive Quarry: A Scientific Appraisal of Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited in discussion as representing skeptical position on elusiveness of psychic phenomena. Sheldrake cites Hyman's argument that phenomena should be treated with suspicion if they cannot be demonstrated repeatedly. Foundational skeptical text that articulates the 'elusiveness problem' central to psi debate.

papers_wanted.title: The Psychology of the Psychic
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited in introduction as representing skeptical explanation for telephone telepathy experiences (selective memory + chance coincidence). Important skeptical source that Sheldrake explicitly tests against. Second edition published by Prometheus Books.

papers_wanted.title: Correlation of Global Events with REG Data: An Internet-Based, Nonlocal Anomalies Experiment
papers_wanted.why_needed: Early GCP methodology paper describing the Internet-based RNG network and initial event correlation analyses. Cited by Bancel & Nelson (2008) as reference 15. Important precursor to the full 9-year analysis.

papers_wanted.title: Phenomenal Characteristics of Memories for Perceived and Imagined Autobiographical Events
papers_wanted.why_needed: Original validation of the Memory Characteristics Questionnaire (MCQ) - the gold standard instrument used by Thonnard (2013), Palmieri (2014), Cassol (2018), and Moore & Greyson (2017) to assess NDE memory phenomenology. Foundational methodology paper for the entire NDE memory research line.

papers_wanted.title: Risk Management in Hypnotic Recall of Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Safety guidelines for hypnotic recall of NDEs, explicitly followed by Palmieri et al. (2014) in their methodology. Important for understanding ethical protocols in NDE research and hypnosis-based memory enhancement.

papers_wanted.title: Global Resonance of Consciousness: Princess Diana and Mother Teresa
papers_wanted.why_needed: Early proto-GCP experiment using ad hoc RNG network during Princess Diana funeral (Sept 1997); cited by Nelson 2014 GCP invited article as key precursor showing field consciousness effects could extend from small groups to millions; foundational to the GCP research program

papers_wanted.title: Parapsychology: The Controversial Science
papers_wanted.why_needed: Skeptical perspective on parapsychology cited by Nelson (2001). Important for controversy #10 (meta-debate) and provides critical counterpoint to psi claims. Broughton's critiques are frequently cited in skeptical literature.

papers_wanted.title: The Global Brain
papers_wanted.why_needed: Conceptual precursor to GCP, exploring the global consciousness concept. Cited by Nelson (2001) in discussion of noosphere and global mind. Important for historical context of collective consciousness theories.

papers_wanted.title: Anomalous Organization of Random Events by Group Consciousness: Two Exploratory Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion paper to Nelson et al. (1996) in same JSE issue. Independent replication of group consciousness REG effects. Cited in Nelson Discussion as beginning of replication efforts. Important for GCP methodological development.

papers_wanted.title: Precognitive Remote Viewing in the Chicago Area: A Replication of the Stanford Experiment
papers_wanted.why_needed: Historical precursor to PEAR remote perception program. First formal replication of SRI remote viewing experiments that motivated the entire PEAR program. Cited in Nelson introduction as original basis for PEAR remote perception research.

papers_wanted.title: A PK Test with Electronic Equipment
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper that introduced electronic REG testing for psychokinesis — the paradigm used by PEAR and virtually every subsequent PK experiment in the library. Cited by Jahn et al. 1987, Radin 1989, Bosch 2006 meta-analysis, and many others.

papers_wanted.title: Remembering pride and shame: Self-enhancement and the phenomenology of autobiographical memory
papers_wanted.why_needed: This paper presents the adapted MCQ version actually used in martial_2017_intensity_memory and other Liège group NDE studies (thonnard_2013_nde_memory_reality). The adaptation encompasses 16 rating scales assessing phenomenological memory characteristics. Needed to properly cite the methodology used in multiple library papers.

papers_wanted.title: Comunicación Directa y Potencial Transferido [Direct Communication and Transferred Potential]
papers_wanted.journal: Rivista Intercontinental de Psicología y Educación
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to the 1994 transferred potential paper — first demonstration of transferred potentials between subjects in Faraday chambers at 3m distance. Essential for understanding the development of the paradigm and for comparison with the 14.5m replication.

papers_wanted.title: The Self-Aware Universe: How Consciousness Creates the Material World
papers_wanted.why_needed: Provides the idealist quantum consciousness theoretical framework used to interpret the transferred potential experiments. Goswami is co-author on the 1994 paper. Relevant to quantum models of consciousness debate in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Critique of the PEAR Remote-Viewing Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: The primary skeptical critique of PEAR's remote perception program. Directly addressed in Dunne & Jahn 2003 and Dobyns et al. 1992 rebuttal. Essential for the remote viewing controversy and meta-debate on methodology.

papers_wanted.title: Response to Hansen, Utts, and Markwick: Statistical and Methodological Problems of the PEAR Remote Viewing Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: The PEAR team's formal rebuttal to Hansen et al.'s critique. Companion to hansen_utts_1992_critique_pear; together they form the core exchange in the PEAR remote viewing controversy.

papers_wanted.title: Precognitive Remote Perception: Replication of Remote Viewing
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct replication study of the PEAR precognitive remote perception experiments. Referenced in Dunne & Jahn 2003 as part of the formal binary database. Important for replication evidence in remote viewing.

papers_wanted.title: Evidence That Anomalous Statistical Influence Depends on the Details of the Random Process
papers_wanted.why_needed: Initial publication of MegaREG results showing reversed intentional effect in high-density trials. Cited by dobyns_2004_megareg as the first report of the unexpected findings. Foundational for understanding the full experimental program.

papers_wanted.title: Dissociation in People Who Have Near-Death Experiences: Out of Their Bodies or Out of Their Minds?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Greyson's Lancet paper linking dissociative tendencies to NDE occurrence — the primary psychological-model evidence cited in greyson_2003_incidence_nde_cardiac. Key for understanding whether NDEs reflect a dissociative defense mechanism.

papers_wanted.title: A Prospective Analysis of Near-Death Experiences in Cardiac Arrest Patients
papers_wanted.why_needed: Third prospective cardiac arrest NDE study published alongside van Lommel 2001 and Parnia 2001; found 23% NDE incidence among 30 survivors. Cited in greyson_2003_incidence_nde_cardiac as the highest-incidence comparable study.

papers_wanted.title: The Soal-Goldney Experiments with Basil Shackleton: New Evidence of Data Manipulation
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark discovery that Soal manipulated data in what had been considered the strongest evidence for telepathy. Hyman (1996) uses it as a key example of how seemingly impeccable parapsychological evidence was later discovered to be fraudulent. Foundational to Controversy #10 (meta-debate on fraud and methodology).

papers_wanted.title: Experimenter Effects in Behavioral Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational text on experimenter expectancy effects—the key mechanism Kennedy (1981) invokes to explain how skeptics' biases produce errors toward chance. Relevant to multiple library papers on experimenter effects (Kennedy 2017, Schlitz 2006) and the meta-debate controversy (#10).

papers_wanted.title: Personal Values and ESP Scores
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Abnormal and Social Psychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Original sheep-goat effect paper establishing that ESP believers score higher than skeptics—the foundational finding that Wilson (1964) replicated (p<.005) before sabotaging with an underpowered follow-up, as documented in Kennedy (1981). The sheep-goat paradigm is referenced across multiple library papers.

papers_wanted.title: Why Science Is Not Necessarily Self-Correcting
papers_wanted.why_needed: Argues that science's self-correcting mechanisms are insufficient, directly relevant to the meta-debate controversy (#10) about whether parapsychology can rely on replication and meta-analysis to converge on truth. Cited by Button et al. 2013 and by Ioannidis' own 2005 paper already in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Consequences of Prejudice Against the Null Hypothesis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Classic paper on publication bias against null results, foundational to understanding why parapsychological meta-analyses may overestimate effects. Cited by Button et al. 2013; directly relevant to meta-debate controversy (#10) and to interpreting all meta-analyses in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Negative Results Are Disappearing from Most Disciplines and Countries
papers_wanted.why_needed: Empirically demonstrates that negative results are vanishing from published literature across disciplines. Relevant to debates about whether parapsychology's positive publication rate is anomalous or typical of science broadly. Cited by Button et al. 2013.

papers_wanted.title: Analytic cognitive style predicts religious and paranormal belief
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key empirical study linking analytic cognitive style to reduced paranormal belief, cited by Gray & Gallo 2016 and foundational to the cognitive-differences literature. Would strengthen the library's coverage of the psychology of belief.

papers_wanted.title: Belief in psychic ability and the misattribution hypothesis: a qualitative review
papers_wanted.why_needed: Important qualitative review of misattribution as explanation for psychic belief, by two prominent researchers already represented in the library (Wiseman, Watt). Directly cited by Gray & Gallo 2016 and connects to the analytical-thinking hypothesis.

papers_wanted.title: Psychic and spiritual experiences, health, well-being, and meaning in life
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational study on the relationship between paranormal experiences and well-being, cited by Gray & Gallo 2016 and by multiple Kennedy papers already in the library. Key precursor to Kennedy's extensive body of work on psi and meaning.

papers_wanted.title: Publication Bias in the Social Sciences: Unlocking the File Drawer
papers_wanted.why_needed: Empirical study of the TESS file-drawer providing the publication probability function used in Bierman et al. 2016 simulations. Key evidence for the meta-debate controversy (#10) on how publication bias distorts meta-analytic results.

papers_wanted.title: Questionable Research Practices Revisited
papers_wanted.why_needed: Replication and extension of John et al. 2012 QRP survey, providing repetition rates that distinguish admission rates from actual prevalence. Used by Bierman et al. 2016 to calibrate QRP frequency parameters.

papers_wanted.title: Methodological Criticisms of Parapsychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational methodological critique of parapsychology cited across the library's methodology papers. Kennedy (2017) references it as an early advocate for fraud prevention standards. Essential for Controversy #10 (meta-debate) as a systematic catalog of methodological weaknesses in psi research.

papers_wanted.title: Hansel's Ghost: Resurrection of the Experimenter Fraud Hypothesis in Parapsychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Editorial that Kennedy (2017) directly responds to — argues that fraud prevention measures create a 'paranoid work environment' and that discussing fraud about specific studies is unethical without strong evidence. Without this paper, the Kennedy 2017 rebuttal in the library lacks its target.

papers_wanted.title: Security Measures in an Automated Ganzfeld System
papers_wanted.why_needed: Demonstrates practical implementation of fraud prevention measures in ganzfeld telepathy experiments — the very standard Kennedy (2017) advocates. Bridges Controversy #1 (ganzfeld) and #10 (meta-debate). Co-authored by both proponents (Radin, Morris) and a skeptic (Wiseman), making it a rare collaborative security design.

papers_wanted.title: The Case Against Psi
papers_wanted.journal: Parapsychology: A Handbook for the 21st Century
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major skeptical overview chapter in the definitive 21st-century parapsychology handbook. Kennedy (2017) cites Stokes as sharing his view that substantial undetected fraud has likely occurred. Important skeptical counterpoint for Controversy #10 and the library's methodology discussion.

papers_wanted.title: Options for Prospective Meta-Analysis and Introduction of Registration-Based Prospective Meta-Analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Proposes registration-based prospective meta-analysis as a confirmatory alternative to retrospective meta-analyses in psi research. Directly cited by Kennedy (2024) as a preferred methodology for synthesizing preregistered studies. Companion to Watt & Kennedy (2015) study registry paper already in catalog.

papers_wanted.title: Lessons and Recommendations from a Research Audit for the Transparent Psi Project (TPP)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Reports on quality control issues discovered during an audit of the Transparent Psi Project, the largest preregistered psi study to date. Cited by Kennedy (2024) as providing examples and recommendations for research quality control. Complements Kekecs et al. (2023) TPP already on wishlist.

papers_wanted.title: Retrospective Median Power, False Positive Meta-Analysis and Large-Scale Replication
papers_wanted.why_needed: Found that Bem's (2011) precognition meta-analysis had median retrospective power of 7.7%, and that false-positive rates as high as 50% are consistent with available data. Directly relevant to evaluating psi meta-analyses. Cited by Kennedy (2024) and connects to Ioannidis (2005) already in catalog.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness-Related Effects in Random Physical Systems
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational meta-analysis of consciousness-related anomalies in random physical systems (RNGs/REGs). Cited by Braud & Schlitz 1991 as key background evidence for mind-matter interaction. Widely referenced across the PK literature in this library.

papers_wanted.title: Experiments in Distant Influence
papers_wanted.why_needed: Historical precursor to the entire DMILS research program. Braud & Schlitz 1991 explicitly states their experiments are 'conceptual replications of human distant mental influence experiments conducted many years ago in the Soviet Union (by Bekhterev, Vasiliev).' Essential context for Controversy #5.

papers_wanted.title: Survey of Spiritual Healing Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive survey of ~100 published experiments on mental influence of biological systems (bacteria, yeast, plants, animals, cells). Cited by Braud & Schlitz 1991 as evidence for the breadth of the distant healing phenomenon. Fills a gap in the library's healing literature.

papers_wanted.title: Intersubject EEG Coherence: Is Consciousness a Field?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to the transferred potential paradigm: reported that meditation by one group at a distance changed EEG coherence in another group. Cited by Grinberg-Zylberbaum (1992) as evidence that brain coherence in one subject affects distant brains. Relevant to the EEG brain-brain correlation debate and telepathy controversy.

papers_wanted.title: El Espacio y la Conciencia
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical work introducing the syntergic theory of consciousness and neuronal fields. Cited by grinberg_zylberbaum_1987_interhemispheric and subsequent papers in this research program. Required to understand the theoretical framework underlying the interhemispheric correlation paradigm and transferred potential experiments.
sources_not_yet_tried: library search for Spanish-language consciousness books
contact Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México library

papers_wanted.title: The Model of Pragmatic Information (MPI)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical model predicting decline effects and sign-inversion in psi replication attempts. Discussed extensively in Rabeyron 2014 and relevant to multiple library papers addressing replication difficulties. The 2007 Lucadou papers in the wishlist build on this original formulation.

papers_wanted.title: A Telekinetic Effect on Plant Growth
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational healing intention experiment: first controlled study showing a healer's influence on plant growth via treated water. Cited as a precursor by multiple healing/intention papers in the library (Radin 2006, Braud & Schlitz 1991). Fills the historical gap in the healing research lineage.

papers_wanted.title: Healing with Water
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine
papers_wanted.why_needed: The original peer-reviewed statement of the water crystal hypothesis that Radin et al. (2006) and Radin et al. (2008) tested under controlled conditions. Without this paper, the library has the tests but not the original claim.

papers_wanted.title: Precognition: The Only Form of Psi?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical paper arguing all psi phenomena reduce to precognition. Central to understanding the DAT (Decision Augmentation Theory) framework. Cited in Mossbridge & Radin (2018) review and relevant to the library's precognition controversy and theoretical foundations.

papers_wanted.title: A Critique of the Parapsychological Random Number Generator Meta-Analyses of Radin and Nelson
papers_wanted.why_needed: The direct target of radin_2006_assessing (a point-by-point reply). Without this critique, the defense paper lacks its counterpart. Fills the skeptical side of the RNG/PK meta-analytic debate (Controversy #8).

papers_wanted.title: Meta-Analysis of Mind-Matter Experiments: A Statistical Modeling Perspective
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of four independent re-examinations of the RNG meta-analytic data discussed in radin_2006_assessing. Provides a statistical modeling critique distinct from Bösch et al.'s approach. Fills a gap in the skeptical/methodological critique literature for Controversy #8.

papers_wanted.title: Publication Bias: The 'File-Drawer' Problem in Scientific Inference
papers_wanted.why_needed: Develops the filedrawer estimation method (using non-zero mean for unpublished studies) that Schub applied against the Radin & Nelson meta-analyses. Foundational methodology paper for the publication bias debate in parapsychology (Controversy #10).

papers_wanted.title: Experimenter Effects in Parapsychological Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational early review of experimenter effects in psi research, cited by Kennedy 2020 and likely by multiple other Kennedy papers in the library. Key historical reference for understanding the experimenter-effect theme.

papers_wanted.title: Personality and Motivations to Believe, Misbelieve, and Disbelieve in Paranormal Phenomena
papers_wanted.why_needed: Addresses attitude polarization toward psi — a unique angle not covered by other Kennedy papers in the library. Supports Conclusion 7 of Kennedy 2020 about personality-driven belief/disbelief patterns.

papers_wanted.title: Do People Guide Psi or Does Psi Guide People? Evidence and Implications from Life and Lab
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper for Kennedy's 'purpose of psi' thesis — argues psi may serve life-guiding functions rather than utilitarian goals. Cited by Kennedy 2020 (Conclusions 1, 4, 6) and likely by kennedy_2006_spirituality.

papers_wanted.title: Experimenter Effects and the Remote Facilitation of Attention Focusing: Two Studies and the Discovery of an Artifact
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key example of software artifact producing false positive psi results — directly cited by Kennedy (2016) as evidence for why software validation is essential. Foundational case study for the software validation argument in methodology debates.

papers_wanted.title: The New Statistics: Why and How
papers_wanted.why_needed: Widely influential advocacy for abandoning hypothesis testing in favor of effect-size estimation — Kennedy (2016) critiques this as a 'major retreat from the basic principles of good science.' Central to the statistical methods debate relevant to evaluating psi evidence.

papers_wanted.title: Experimenter Misconduct in Parapsychology: Analysis Manipulation and Fraud
papers_wanted.why_needed: Detailed analysis of fraud in parapsychological research, the primary source behind Kennedy's fraud-prevention recommendations. Directly cited multiple times by Kennedy (2016) and Kennedy (2017). Fills the gap between Akers (1984) and Kennedy (2017) on fraud in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Recollections of Death: A Medical Investigation
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational medical investigation of NDEs cited by Greyson 1983. Sabom was a cardiologist who systematically studied NDE reports. Essential historical precursor to quantitative NDE research. Cited alongside Ring 1980 as key early work.

papers_wanted.title: The Challenge of Experimenter Psi
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key review of experimenter psi effects cited by Kennedy 2013 and others. Directly relevant to the experimenter effects theme and the question of whether experimenters can use psi to influence their own experiments — a central issue in the meta-debate.

papers_wanted.title: Exploratory Analysis of the Global Consciousness Project Data: 15 Years
papers_wanted.why_needed: Most comprehensive overview of 15 years of GCP formal results; directly extended by radin_2023_anomalous. Would fill gap as the definitive summary of the GCP event-based experiment before Radin's full-database reanalysis. Relevant to Controversy #8.

papers_wanted.title: Exploring Relationships Between Random Physical Events and Mass Human Attention: Asking for Whom the Bell Tolls
papers_wanted.why_needed: Early independent analysis correlating GCP RNG data with daily news events; cited by radin_2023_anomalous (ref 12) as precursor to the full-database reanalysis approach. Same research program as radin_2023_anomalous. Relevant to Controversy #8.

papers_wanted.title: Belief in the paranormal: A review of the empirical literature
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by dean_2022 as the last major review before their systematic synthesis. Foundational baseline for the cognitive deficits hypothesis. Dean et al. explicitly position their review as updating Irwin's work after 30 years. Essential for understanding historical development of the cognitive differences research program.
sources_not_yet_tried: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research archives

papers_wanted.title: On the reception and detection of pseudo-profound bullshit
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by dean_2022 (ref 14). Highly influential paper on individual differences in receptivity to meaningless but impressive-sounding claims. Connects paranormal belief to broader literature on reasoning, analytic thinking, and epistemic rationality. Important for understanding cognitive style correlates.
sources_not_yet_tried: Judgment and Decision Making journal website

papers_wanted.title: Enhancing Human Performance: Issues, Theories, and Techniques
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark NRC/NAS report commissioned by the US Army that evaluated parapsychology alongside other performance-enhancement techniques. Concluded there was no scientific justification for psi. Foundational skeptical reference cited by Reber & Alcock (2019) and central to the institutional history of psi evaluation. Fills a gap in the library's skeptical/methodology holdings.

papers_wanted.title: Mediumship and Survival: A Century of Investigations
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational reference book on mediumship research, cited by nearly every mediumship-related paper in the library including Kelly 2010, Braude 1992, and Beischel 2015. Provides the most comprehensive historical review of 100 years of mediumship investigation.

papers_wanted.title: Super-Psi and the Survivalist Interpretation of Mediumship
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key philosophical analysis arguing that arguments against super-psi are a 'double-edged sword' that equally undercut the survival hypothesis. Directly engaged by Kelly 2010 and central to the survival vs. super-psi controversy (#7).

papers_wanted.title: Immortal Remains: The Evidence for Life After Death
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive philosophical evaluation of survival evidence (mediumship, reincarnation, apparitions). Extends Braude 1992 already in the library. Cited by Kelly 2010 and multiple other library papers on survival. Key reference for the survival vs. super-psi debate.

papers_wanted.title: Unlearned Language: New Studies in Xenoglossy
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key evidence for responsive xenoglossy (the Sharada/Uttara case) that Braude 1992 critiques at length. Central to the survival-vs-super-psi debate. Also cited by Kelly 2010 and Delorme 2021.

papers_wanted.title: What Would Constitute Conclusive Evidence of Survival After Death?
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Formulated the foundational principle that skills are incommunicable paranormally — the key axiom challenged by Braude 1992. Central philosophical reference in the survival-vs-super-psi debate.

papers_wanted.title: The Limits of Influence: Psychokinesis and the Philosophy of Science
papers_wanted.why_needed: Braude's philosophical examination of PK evidence including physical mediumship, which he cites as the best evidence for large-scale psi. Foundational to the super-psi argument in braude_1992_survival.

papers_wanted.title: Parapsychology and the Unconscious
papers_wanted.why_needed: Contains Eisenbud's depth-psychological analysis of the Cagliostro persona — the model case Braude 1992 uses to demonstrate how survival evidence dissolves under psychodynamic scrutiny. Key reference for depth-psychological approaches to mediumship.

papers_wanted.title: Bayesian t Tests for Accepting and Rejecting the Null Hypothesis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper introducing the JZS default Bayes factor for t-tests, which is the basis for all Bayesian reanalyses of Bem's data (Wagenmakers et al. 2011, Rouder & Morey 2011, Rouder et al. 2013). Also widely adopted in broader psychology as an alternative to null-hypothesis significance testing. Fills methodology gap.

papers_wanted.title: The File Drawer Problem and Tolerance for Null Results
papers_wanted.why_needed: Coined the 'file drawer problem' — foundational to every publication bias debate in psi meta-analyses. Cited by OSC (2015), and almost certainly by multiple library papers on ganzfeld and Bem meta-analyses. Essential for Controversy #10 (meta-debate).

papers_wanted.title: Investigating Variation in Replicability: A 'Many Labs' Replication Project
papers_wanted.why_needed: Complementary 'narrow-and-deep' replication project to the OSC's 'broad-and-shallow' approach. Replicated 13 effects across 36 labs. Provides context for evaluating whether psi non-replications reflect normal scientific variation or something specific to parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: The Statistical Power of Abnormal-Social Psychological Research: A Review
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Abnormal and Social Psychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Seminal paper demonstrating chronically low statistical power in psychology. Historical precursor to the replication crisis; directly relevant to debates about whether psi studies are underpowered. Cited by OSC (2015) and Button et al. (2013), both in catalog.

papers_wanted.title: An Experimentally Testable Model for Spontaneous Psi Events I. Extrasensory Events
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational to DAT: May et al. (1995) describe DAT as 'a logical and formal extension of Stanford's elegant PMIR model.' Stanford's psi-mediated instrumental response (PMIR) model is one of the most cited phenomenological theories in parapsychology and a direct precursor to DAT.

papers_wanted.title: Applications of Decision Augmentation Theory
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion paper to May et al. (1995) DAT theory paper already in the library. Contains the retrospective empirical tests of DAT predictions against actual RNG data. Essential for evaluating whether DAT's predictions hold up against real experimental data.

papers_wanted.title: A New Theory of the Relationship of Mind and Matter
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical paper applying Bohm's ontological interpretation of QM to the mind-matter problem. Directly cited by Josephson & Pallikari-Viras (1991) as proposing that nonlocal effects are possible but fragile. Essential precursor to the quantum-consciousness framework papers in the library (Walach 2014, Tressoldi 2010, Bierman 2003).

papers_wanted.title: Near-Death Experiences with Reports of Meeting Deceased People
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational empirical analysis directly cited by Greyson (2010) that contrasts the expectation hypothesis with the survival hypothesis for NDE encounters with deceased persons. Essential precursor to the Peak in Darien argument in controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Do Any Near-Death Experiences Provide Evidence for the Survival of Human Personality after Death? Relevant Features and Illustrative Case Reports
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Greyson (2010) by the same research group, systematically evaluating which NDE features bear on the survival question. Greyson's Peak in Darien review builds directly on this framework. Central to controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: At the Hour of Death
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational book-length study of deathbed visions, providing systematic cross-cultural data (US and India) on what dying persons report seeing. Cited by Greyson (2010) and multiple NDE researchers. Historical cornerstone of the deathbed vision research program.

papers_wanted.title: Decision Augmentation Theory: Applications to the Random Number Generator Database
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Jahn et al. (1997) as incompatible with their findings.
