Jahn 1997 shows deterministic pseudorandom sources yield null results while true random sources show effects - DAT predicts source type should be irrelevant. Critical theoretical paper that Jahn 1997 empirically constrains.

papers_wanted.title: PK Tests with a High-Speed Random Number Generator
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Jahn et al. (1997) as the foundational work PEAR was attempting to replicate with improved methodology. Schmidt pioneered high-speed RNG PK experiments. Historical precursor to the entire PEAR program.

papers_wanted.title: Operator-Related Anomalies in a Random Mechanical Cascade
papers_wanted.why_needed: The RMC experiment is a major component of Jahn et al. (1997)'s Table 2 results. 9000 polystyrene spheres through 330 pegs - macroscopic mechanical PK demonstration. Shows effect generalizes beyond electronic REGs.

papers_wanted.title: Comparison of PK Action on Two Different Random Number Generators
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational experiment for the goal-oriented psi hypothesis. Schmidt's direct comparison of single-event vs. 100-event majority-vote PK trials under double-blind conditions is the most definitive empirical test of goal-oriented psi. Cited by kennedy_1995_goal_oriented, kennedy_2001_psi_elusive, and schmidt_1987_strange. Central to methodology debates about whether psi bypasses statistical redundancy.

papers_wanted.title: Redundancy in Psi Information: Implications for the Goal-Oriented Psi Hypothesis and for the Application of Psi
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to kennedy_1995_goal_oriented. First systematic analysis of redundancy in psi tasks and its implications for majority-vote signal enhancement. Provides the detailed calculations and data tables that the 1995 paper builds on. Essential for understanding Kennedy's research program on goal-oriented psi.

papers_wanted.title: Exploring the Limits of Science and Beyond: Research Strategy and Status
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Kennedy's goal-oriented psi hypothesis and the 2004 proposal for pivotal studies. Introduces the concept that psi may not conform to standard experimental assumptions, which Kennedy develops across his subsequent research program. Cited by kennedy_2004_proposal and kennedy_1995_goal_oriented.

papers_wanted.title: Report of a Workshop on Experimental Parapsychology
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: U.S. government assessment of experimental parapsychology that proposed the proponent-skeptic committee approach Kennedy builds on in kennedy_2004_proposal. Historically foundational document for psi methodology debates. Published in JASPR vol. 83, pp. 317-339.

papers_wanted.title: Bayesian Versus Orthodox Statistics: Which Side Are You On?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Influential tutorial on Bayesian hypothesis testing frequently cited in psi methodology debates. Referenced by Wagenmakers et al. (2012) and relevant to understanding why Bayesian methods are preferred over p-values in contemporary psi research evaluation.

papers_wanted.title: HARKing: Hypothesizing After the Results Are Known
papers_wanted.why_needed: Coined the widely-used term 'HARKing' for post-hoc hypothesis generation disguised as confirmatory. Foundational reference for the methodology debate and directly relevant to critiques of exploratory psi research presented as confirmatory.

papers_wanted.title: The Strength of Evidence Versus the Power of Belief: Are We All Bayesians?
papers_wanted.journal: Paper presented at ICOTS-8, Ljubljana, Slovenia
papers_wanted.why_needed: Bayesian meta-analysis of 56 telepathy experiments yielding Cohen's h ~ 0.18. Foundational for calibrating knowledge-based priors in psi Bayesian analyses (cited by Bem et al. 2011 as basis for their prior). Connects to Controversies #1 (ganzfeld) and #10 (meta-debate).

papers_wanted.title: Statistical Aspects of ESP
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational statistical critique of ESP experimental methodology; first formal analysis of optional stopping in psi experiments. Cited by Wagenmakers (2007) as demonstrating that optional stopping can always reject any true null hypothesis. Directly relevant to methodology controversies (#10) and the historical timeline of statistical critiques of psi.

papers_wanted.title: The Earth Is Round (p < .05)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Classic and widely-cited critique of NHST in psychology; cited by Wagenmakers (2007) and relevant to understanding the statistical methodology debates around psi research. One of the most influential papers arguing that p values are misunderstood and misused.

papers_wanted.title: Bayesian Statistical Inference for Psychological Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper introducing Bayesian methods to psychology; established that stopping rules are irrelevant to data interpretation under Bayesian framework. Directly quoted by Wagenmakers (2007) on the legitimacy of optional stopping. Key to understanding why Bayesian reanalyses of psi data (Rouder & Morey 2011, Wagenmakers 2011) use stopping-rule-independent methods.

papers_wanted.title: An Exploratory Test for an Excess of Significant Findings
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational methodological paper that developed the test for excess significance (TES) used by Francis (2012) to demonstrate publication bias in Bem's psi experiments. Central tool in the meta-debate about whether psi findings are contaminated by publication bias.

papers_wanted.title: Does Paranormal Perception Occur in Near-Death Experiences?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major skeptical critique of veridical perception claims in NDEs, directly cited by Greyson (2011). Would fill a gap as a critical counterpoint to the pro-NDE evidence in controversy #7. Currently the library lacks Augustine's arguments despite him being one of the most cited NDE skeptics.

papers_wanted.title: Veridical Perception in Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.journal: The Handbook of Near-Death Experiences (book chapter)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Systematic review of 93 published veridical OBE reports finding 92% completely accurate. Most comprehensive data source for veridical NDE perception claims, cited extensively in NDE literature. Foundational to controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Cardiac Arrest and Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Skeptical analysis of NDE claims during cardiac arrest by an anesthesiologist. Provides physiological counter-arguments to Greyson's enhanced-mentation-during-arrest claims. Would balance the predominantly pro-NDE content in controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Terminal Lucidity in Patients with Chronic Schizophrenia and Dementia: A Survey of the Literature
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease
papers_wanted.why_needed: Documents unexplained return of mental clarity before death in patients with severe brain damage — terminal lucidity. Provides additional evidence for the mind-brain dissociation argument central to controversy #7. By Greyson and colleague.

papers_wanted.title: Methodology for Confirmatory Experiments on Physiological Measures of Precognitive Anticipation
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited in Mossbridge et al. (2015) as an important reference for handling expectation bias in presentiment experiments. Provides methodological recommendations for confirmatory precognition research. Would strengthen the methodology coverage in the presentiment controversy.

papers_wanted.title: An Experimental Analysis of Facilitated Communication
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key early controlled study finding facilitator influence in FC; one of the studies cited by Weiler & Woollacott (2025) as potentially reinterpretable. Fills the Con side of controversy #9 with original experimental evidence.

papers_wanted.title: Interrogating Neurotypical Bias in Facilitated Communication, Rapid Prompting Method, and Spelling 2 Communicate Through a Humanistic Lens
papers_wanted.why_needed: Recent review examining how neurotypical bias shapes FC/RPM/S2C research and clinical judgment. Directly relevant to controversy #9 and the presuming-competence framework cited by Weiler & Woollacott (2025).

papers_wanted.title: Geomagnetic Fluctuations and Free-Response Anomalous Cognition: A New Understanding
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion to spottiswoode_1997_apparent (sidereal time effect). Shows geomagnetic fluctuations are lowest during the same sidereal time window when RV performance peaks, providing a physical correlate. Cited by lee_2008_remote_viewing_futures and relevant to the remote viewing evidence base.

papers_wanted.title: Mental, Behavioural and Physiological Nonlocal Correlations Within the Generalized Quantum Theory Framework
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct predecessor in the GQT research program testing nonlocal correlations across mental, behavioral, and physiological domains. Cited by walach_2020_evidence as part of the same theoretical lineage. Fills the gap between the foundational GQT theory (walach_2014_entanglement) and the experimental replication (walach_2020_evidence).

papers_wanted.title: Review of the Psychoenergetic Research Conducted at SRI International (1973-1988)
papers_wanted.why_needed: The comprehensive internal review of the entire SRI remote viewing program (1973-1988), providing aggregate effect sizes and individual viewer performance data that Utts's 1996 CIA-commissioned assessment directly builds upon. Foundational document for the Stargate literature in this library.

papers_wanted.title: An Experiment with the Icelandic Medium Hafsteinn Björnsson
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational mediumship experiment that introduced the sitter-isolated design (sitter present but visually/acoustically separated from medium). Cited by Kelly 2010 as a key precursor to modern mediumship methodology. 4 of 10 sitters ranked their own reading first (p < .01). Directly relevant to controversy #6 (mediumship).

papers_wanted.title: The Out-of-Body Experience: Disturbed Self-Processing at the Temporo-Parietal Junction
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key neuroscience paper proposing that out-of-body experiences result from disturbed multisensory processing at the temporo-parietal junction. Cited in Facco et al. (2015) and widely referenced in NDE debates. Provides the leading materialist account of OBEs, important for the Con side of controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Human Personality and Its Survival of Bodily Death
papers_wanted.why_needed: Myers' magnum opus published posthumously, introducing concepts like 'telepathy' and 'subliminal self' that became central to parapsychology. Rhine's experimental program was designed to test Myers' theoretical framework. Critical historical foundation.

papers_wanted.title: La Suggestion Mentale et le Calcul des Probabilités
papers_wanted.why_needed: First application of probability mathematics to telepathy experiments, as noted by Rhine in his Appendix. Historical precursor to Rhine's statistical methodology. Richet later won Nobel Prize in Physiology/Medicine (1913).

papers_wanted.title: Scientific Utopia: II. Restructuring Incentives and Practices to Promote Truth Over Publishability
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Gelman & Loken (2013) as a positive example of pre-publication replication. This paper introduced the two-stage original+replication design that Gelman recommends as a solution to the garden of forking paths problem. Important for understanding the methodological reform response to the Bem controversy.

papers_wanted.title: Women Are More Likely to Wear Red or Pink at Peak Fertility
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of three main case studies in Gelman & Loken (2013). Used as an example of how researcher degrees of freedom and data-contingent analysis choices can produce spurious statistically significant results. Adding this would allow readers to see the original paper that Gelman critiques.

papers_wanted.title: The Fluctuating Female Vote: Politics, Religion, and the Ovulatory Cycle
papers_wanted.why_needed: Second main case study in Gelman & Loken (2013). Discussed as an example of interaction testing and data-contingent analysis choices. The paper claims ovulation affects women's political preferences differently for single vs. married women. Important for understanding the specific claims Gelman critiques.

papers_wanted.title: Stimulating illusory own-body perceptions
papers_wanted.why_needed: Nature paper demonstrating that electrical stimulation of the right angular gyrus can induce out-of-body experiences. Foundational study for neurobiological OBE explanations; cited by Bokkon 2013 and relevant to controversy #7 (Con side — OBE as brain-generated phenomenon).

papers_wanted.title: Surges of electroencephalogram activity at the time of death: a case series
papers_wanted.why_needed: Reports transient high-frequency EEG surges (1-5 min) in dying patients after life-support withdrawal. Key evidence that neural activity can surge during the dying process, directly relevant to NDE mechanisms. Cited by Bokkon 2013; fills gap in empirical NDE neuroscience evidence for controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: A Computational Expectation Bias as Revealed by Simulations of Presentiment Experiments
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the 45th Annual Convention of the Parapsychological Association
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational methodological critique of presentiment experiments, demonstrating how expectation bias from sequential trial designs can produce artifacts mimicking PAA. Directly addressed in Mossbridge et al. 2014 and central to the methodological debate in Controversy #3.

papers_wanted.title: The Ganzfeld Novice: Four Predictors of Initial Psi Performance
papers_wanted.why_needed: Reports confirmation attempt of autoganzfeld internal effects with novice participants. Milton & Wiseman (1999) critique this paper, noting that the mental discipline effect was nonsignificant. Important for understanding the internal effects debate.

papers_wanted.title: Information Transmission in Remote Viewing Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct critique of Puthoff & Targ's SRI remote viewing experiments (puthoff_1976_perceptual, targ_1974_information). Marks & Kammann argued that cues in the transcripts (references to time of day, previous experiments) allowed judges to match without psi. This is the primary skeptical challenge to the foundational SRI remote viewing data and essential for balanced coverage of Controversy #1/RV research.

papers_wanted.title: Dream Telepathy: Experiments in Nocturnal ESP
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational dream telepathy research from Maimonides Medical Center. This work demonstrated anomalous information transfer during REM sleep and directly inspired Honorton's development of the ganzfeld paradigm (Controversy #1). Cited by puthoff_1976_perceptual and multiple ganzfeld papers in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Intercessory Prayer and Cardiovascular Disease Progression in a Coronary Care Unit Population: A Randomized Controlled Trial
papers_wanted.why_needed: High-profile failure to replicate Byrd 1988 intercessory prayer effects. Fills a critical skeptical gap in Controversy #5 — the library has mostly positive healing studies but needs key null results. Constituent study in Roe et al. 2015 meta-analysis.

papers_wanted.title: The Columbia University 'Miracle' Study: Flawed and Fraud
papers_wanted.why_needed: Exposé of Daniel P. Wirth's fraudulent healing research — the scandal that directly motivated Roe et al. 2015 to exclude Wirth's 5 studies from their meta-analysis. Essential context for understanding why the updated meta-analysis was needed.

papers_wanted.title: Psychophysiologic Correlates of Unconsciousness and Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key source for G-LOC (gravitational loss of consciousness) model of NDEs, analyzing ~1,000 centrifuge episodes across 16 years. Central to Romand & Ehret 2023's unique contribution. Important Con evidence for controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Neurochemical Models of Near-Death Experiences: A Large-Scale Study Based on the Semantic Similarity of Written Reports
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major analysis of 625 NDE-like drug narratives across 165 substances showing ketamine produces the most NDE-similar experiences. Extends the neurochemical evidence base for controversy #7 Con side. Cited by Romand & Ehret 2023.

papers_wanted.title: Experiments Examining the Possibility of Human Intention Interacting with Random Number Generators: A Preliminary Meta-Analysis
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the 45th Convention of the Parapsychological Association
papers_wanted.why_needed: RNG intention meta-analysis (357 experimental studies, 142 controls) finding Stouffer Z=13.09 but weighted Z=2.70 (p=.004) with very small effect (π=.50003) and significant curvilinear relationship between study size and effect size. Key evidence for small-study/publication bias in PK/RNG literature. Speaks to Controversy #8 (GCP/RNG).

papers_wanted.title: Stimulating Illusory Own-Body Perceptions: The Part of the Brain That Can Induce Out-of-Body Experiences Has Been Located
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key skeptical counterargument to veridical OBE claims: shows electrical stimulation of the angular gyrus can produce OBE-like body illusions. Directly critiqued by van Lommel (2013) and central to Controversy #7 on whether NDEs involve genuine perception outside the body.

papers_wanted.title: Mortal Minds: A Biology of the Soul and the Dying Experience
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major skeptical analysis of NDEs proposing physiological explanations (anoxia, drugs, etc.). Directly critiqued by van Lommel (2013). Provides a key opposing perspective for Controversy #7; library currently lacks comprehensive skeptical NDE monographs.

papers_wanted.title: Extra-Sensory Perception After Sixty Years
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational text that established modern experimental parapsychology. Introduced the card-guessing paradigm and statistical methods that Rhine used at Duke University. Cited extensively across the library: Alcock (2003), multiple methodology papers, and historical surveys reference Rhine's work as the starting point of laboratory parapsychology. Fills a historical gap — the library has many papers that critique or build on Rhine but lacks the primary source.

papers_wanted.title: Near-Death Experiences and the Temporal Lobe
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key skeptical study finding higher temporal lobe epileptiform discharges in NDE patients vs controls, critiqued by Facco & Agrillo (2012) who note the control group lacked life-threatening illness. Central to the temporal lobe dysfunction hypothesis in Controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: The Experimental Induction of Out-of-Body Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark Science paper demonstrating experimental induction of OBE-like experiences via visual-tactile manipulation. Foundational for the mechanistic understanding of OBEs and body ownership. Cited by Facco & Agrillo (2012) as evidence that OBE components may involve specific brain regions.

papers_wanted.title: Human Performance Research: An Overview of Meta-Analyses of Interpersonal Expectancy Effects
papers_wanted.why_needed: Meta-analytic overview of experimenter expectancy effects — directly relevant to methodology debates in parapsychology (experimenter effects, demand characteristics). Previously had a bogus download (HTML saved as .pdf from National Academies site).

sources_tried.result: Failed download — HTML landing page saved instead of PDF

papers_wanted.title: Field REG Anomalies in Group Situations
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of three reported successful consciousness-interferometer experiments (Fabry-Perot type) cited in radin_2016_double_slit as independent support. Represents a different optical approach within controversy #4 (double-slit/quantum PK).

papers_wanted.title: Arguing for an Observational Theory of Paranormal Phenomena
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical framework ("Observational Theory") proposing that observer motivation biases quantum event statistics. Cited as the main theoretical model for double-slit and RNG consciousness experiments by radin_2016_double_slit and central to controversy #4.

papers_wanted.title: Weak Quantum Theory: Complementarity and Entanglement in Physics and Beyond
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper establishing the mathematical framework of Generalized Quantum Theory (GQT/Weak Quantum Theory). Defines complementarity and entanglement beyond physics. Cited extensively by walach_2014_entanglement as the core technical reference. Essential for understanding all GQT-based interpretations of psi in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Generalized Quantum Theory: Overview and Latest Developments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Updated overview of GQT formalism with new applications. Cited by walach_2014_entanglement as one of three core technical references. Provides list of GQT applications beyond physics.

papers_wanted.title: Exploring Possible Sender-to-Experimenter Acoustic Leakage in the PRL Autoganzfeld Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key skeptical analysis of possible sensory leakage in the Psychophysical Research Laboratories autoganzfeld experiments. Foundational critique for the ganzfeld debate (controversy #1). Cited by Cardeña 2014 and likely referenced by multiple other library papers.

papers_wanted.title: On the Extraordinary: An Attempt at Clarification
papers_wanted.why_needed: Original source of the 'extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence' principle, frequently invoked in psi debates. Cardeña 2014 argues the principle is typically misapplied. Foundational for the meta-debate (controversy #10).

papers_wanted.title: Kommentar zu Bösch, H., Steinkamp, F., & Boller (2006)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Kugel (2011) as providing statistical correction showing highly significant PK effect after correcting BSB analysis. Important skeptical-era critique that supports Kugel's detailed reanalysis. Missing from library.

papers_wanted.title: Kritischer Kommentar zu einer Meta-Analyse von Bösch, Steinkamp & Boller
papers_wanted.why_needed: Cited by Kugel (2011) as arguing publication bias effect was negligible and results reflect genuine PK effect. Part of the 2006 workshop responses to BSB that Kugel references. Fills gap in coverage of BSB debate.

papers_wanted.title: Parapsychological Research: A Tutorial Review and Critical Appraisal
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major skeptical review of parapsychological research published in Proc. IEEE (74, 823-849). One of the most-cited critical appraisals of psi experiments. Radin & Nelson (1989) engage directly with its methodological criticisms. Would fill the skeptical-methodology gap alongside Diaconis (1978).

papers_wanted.title: Replicability, Experimenter Influence, and Parapsychology: An Empirical Context for the Study of Mind
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of two earlier meta-analytic reviews of RNG experiments that Radin & Nelson (1989) explicitly corroborate (Ref. 17). Foundational precursor to the RNG/PK meta-analytic tradition. Establishes the empirical base that the 1989 meta-analysis extends.

papers_wanted.title: Electronic System Perturbation Techniques
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of two earlier meta-analytic reviews of RNG experiments that Radin & Nelson (1989) explicitly corroborate (Ref. 18). SRI International technical report representing the government-funded research perspective on consciousness-RNG interactions.

papers_wanted.title: On Cumulative Effects and Averaging Artifacts in Randomised S-R Experimental Designs
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the 45th Annual Convention of the Parapsychological Association
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key methodological paper demonstrating how expectation bias and averaging artifacts can produce apparent presentiment effects in randomized stimulus-response designs; directly relevant to Controversy #3 skeptical side and cited by Mossbridge et al. 2012

papers_wanted.title: Exploring the Reliability of the Presentiment Effect
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the 47th Convention of the Parapsychological Association
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent replication attempt from a different lab (high quality score 5.500 in meta-analysis); specifically designed to test reliability of the presentiment paradigm; provides important evidence on reproducibility for Controversy #3

papers_wanted.title: Precognitive Remote Perception
papers_wanted.journal: M.A. thesis, Committee on Human Development, University of Chicago
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational thesis by Brenda Dunne establishing the precognitive remote perception paradigm that became central to PEAR's research program. Cited in Jahn 1982 as ref [161]. Contains the Chicago and European series data presented in the IEEE paper.

papers_wanted.title: The Trickster and the Paranormal
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational book arguing that paranormal phenomena follow the trickster archetype. Extensively cited by Kennedy 2003 as the most developed treatment of psi's evasive nature. Also relevant to the meta-debate (Controversy #10) and decline effects literature. Cited by multiple library papers discussing the elusiveness of psi.

papers_wanted.title: Lessons of History
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key early articulation of the concept that psi is 'actively evasive' — a direct precursor to Kennedy 2003's framework. Beloff proposed that paranormal phenomena may be 'not just elusive, but actively evasive.' Essential for understanding the intellectual lineage of the elusiveness debate.

papers_wanted.title: The Two Faces of Psi: Psi Revealed and Psi Obscured
papers_wanted.journal: In B. Shapin & L. Coly (Eds.), The Repeatability Problem in Parapsychology, Parapsychology Foundation
papers_wanted.why_needed: Described the 'self-obscuring' characteristics of psi and suggested psi occurs more readily in situations with lower methodological quality. A foundational treatment of the elusiveness problem cited by Kennedy 2003 and relevant to the repeatability debate.

papers_wanted.title: Hans in Luck: The Currency of Evidence in Parapsychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Presents the Model of Pragmatic Information (MPI) predicting that psi effects can occur in novel situations but vanish when useful or confirming information is involved. One of three main theoretical frameworks evaluated in Kennedy 2003. Important for the theoretical foundations of psi elusiveness.

papers_wanted.title: Biological Aspects of Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive review of neurobiological mechanisms proposed for NDEs (endorphins, NMDA receptors, temporal lobe stimulation). Cited by van Lommel 2001 and multiple NDE papers in the library. Would fill a gap in the neurobiological-mechanism side of the NDE debate (Controversy #7).

papers_wanted.title: Heading Towards Omega: In Search of the Meaning of the Near-Death Experience
papers_wanted.why_needed: Introduced the life-change inventory used by van Lommel 2001 and subsequent NDE longitudinal studies. Foundational NDE book on the meaning and transformational effects of NDEs. Companion to Ring 1980 (already in wishlist).

papers_wanted.title: Statistical and Methodological Problems of the PEAR Remote Viewing Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational methodological critique of PEAR remote viewing experiments, raising issues about volitional target selection and sampling without replacement. Essential for understanding the strengths and limitations of the PEAR RV evidence base, which is discussed at length in the Baptista et al. 2014 review.

papers_wanted.title: Does Prayer Influence the Success of In Vitro Fertilization-Embryo Transfer?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark three-tier prayer design study (N=219, 50% vs 26% pregnancy rate, P=.0013) cited by Schwartz 2010 as evidence against Decision Augmentation Theory. Important for healing/prayer controversy and methodology debate. Directly relevant to understanding whether therapeutic intention operates via researcher selection or genuine healing effect.

papers_wanted.title: Psychokinetic Control of Bacterial Growth
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Important early study showing intention can affect bacterial colonies both positively and negatively. Cited by Schwartz 2010 as evidence that intention can harm. Relevant to healing-intention research and the question of whether prayer can have negative effects. Nonhuman system avoids confound of extraneous prayer.
sources_not_yet_tried: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research archives

papers_wanted.title: Investigating Methodological Issues in EDA-DMILS: Results from a Pilot Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: The Freiburg group's pilot study that informed the methodology of Schmidt et al. 2004 meta-analysis. Yielded d = 0.41 (medium effect) and met best-evidence criteria; authors planned to exclude it from the meta-analysis but noted its inclusion would have shifted the best-evidence synthesis closer to Model 1. Important for understanding the quality debate.

papers_wanted.title: Near-death experiences in out-of-hospital cardiac arrest survivors: meaningful phenomena or just fantasy of death?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Early skeptical paper questioning NDE validity, proposing fantasy/confabulation explanations. Published in same journal (Resuscitation) as Greyson (2007), representing the skeptical position that Greyson's reliability findings address. Important for controversy #7 balance.

papers_wanted.title: Out of body experiences and their neural basis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key skeptical/neurological perspective on OBEs cited by Parnia 2014 AWARE study (ref 30). Proposes neural mechanisms for OBEs that would challenge the interpretation of OBEs during cardiac arrest as evidence for non-local consciousness. Relevant to controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: The Incidence and Phenomenology of Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of only 4 prospective NDE studies in cardiac arrest survivors worldwide. Reviewed extensively in van_lommel_2013_nonlocal_consciousness alongside the Dutch, Greyson, and Parnia studies. Found 23% NDE incidence (N=39). Fills a gap: the other 3 prospective studies are either in the catalog (Greyson 2003) or wishlist (Parnia 2001), but this one is missing.

papers_wanted.title: The Effect of Carbon Dioxide on Near-Death Experiences in Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest Survivors: A Prospective Observational Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Physiological counter-explanation to NDE survival hypothesis. Found correlation between higher CO2 levels and NDE incidence in 52 cardiac arrest survivors. Explicitly critiqued by van Lommel as confusing correlation with causation. Would provide balance in Controversy #7 as a skeptical/physiological explanation.

papers_wanted.title: A Review of Psychokinesis (PK)
papers_wanted.why_needed: The canonical critical review of PK dice experiments, published in Psychological Bulletin. Cited extensively in radin_1991_effects as the major prior skeptical counterpoint. Would fill a gap as a skeptical/critical paper in the PK literature for Controversy #8.

papers_wanted.title: Theory-Testing in Psychology and Physics: A Methodological Paradox
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational philosophy-of-science paper on why theory-testing in psychology is methodologically weaker than in physics. Introduces the 'strong form' of NHST (testing against a theoretically derived point prediction) that LeBel & Peters 2011 advocate. Cited by multiple library papers including LeBel & Peters 2011, and central to Controversy #10 (meta-debate about psi research soundness).

papers_wanted.title: Not Feeling the Future: A Failed Replication of Retroactive Facilitation of Memory Recall
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Another independent pre-registered replication failure of Bem's retroactive facilitation experiments (Experiment 9). Cited by Ritchie et al. (2012). Adds to the evidence base in Controversy #2 (Bem's Feeling the Future), strengthening the replication failure pattern.

papers_wanted.title: Near-death experiences in cardiac arrest survivors
papers_wanted.why_needed: Skeptical review of NDE evidence in cardiac arrest survivors from a leading skeptical psychologist. Would add balance to the predominantly pro-NDE collection in folder 08. Cited by Thonnard et al. (2013).

papers_wanted.title: On Blowing Trumpets to the Tulips: To Prove or Not to Prove the Null Hypothesis—Comment on Bösch, Steinkamp, and Boller (2006)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion commentary to Radin et al. (2006) in same Psych Bulletin issue responding to BSB PK meta-analysis. Library has BSB (bosch_2006_examining), Radin et al. response (radin_2006_reexamining), and Kugel critique (kugel_2011_faulty_pk_meta) but not this independent statistical evaluation. Wilson & Shadish are prominent meta-analysis methodologists.

papers_wanted.title: Exploring Precognition Using a Repetition Priming Paradigm
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Precognition experiment using repetition priming paradigm. Was in catalog but PDF was wrong file; real paper is Vernon (2015) JSPR 79(2):65-79. Paywalled, no OA version found.

papers_wanted.title: An Investigation of Mediums Who Claim to Give Information About Deceased Persons
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of only a handful of controlled mediumship accuracy studies with blinded sitters. Mixed results: first experiment (n=9) NS, second (n=12) highly significant (p < .0001). Cited by Beischel et al. 2015 as comparative evidence for AIR. Fills gap in Controversy #6 (Mediumship) as an independent test outside the Windbridge group.

papers_wanted.title: CIA-Initiated Remote Viewing Program at Stanford Research Institute
papers_wanted.why_needed: First-hand account of the CIA-initiated RV program at SRI by its co-founder. Foundational document for understanding the Stargate program. Cited by Escola-Gascon 2023 and multiple other library papers on remote viewing. The library has Utts 1996 and Hyman 1996 evaluations but not Puthoff's companion memoir.

papers_wanted.title: Exploring Correlations Between Local Emotional and Global Emotional Events and the Behavior of a Random Number Generator
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent corroboration of the FieldREG/group consciousness phenomenon from outside PEAR. Cited by nelson_1998_fieldreg2 as parallel evidence that group consciousness affects RNG output. Would strengthen controversy #8 (GCP/RNG) by providing independent lab evidence.

papers_wanted.title: An Updated Meta-Analysis of Post-PRL ESP-Ganzfeld Experiments: The Effect of Standardness
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the 43rd Annual Convention of the Parapsychological Association
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion ganzfeld meta-analysis cited by Storm & Ertel (2001) and Bem, Palmer & Broughton (2001). Introduces the 'standardness' moderator variable for post-PRL ganzfeld studies, providing a potential explanation for variable replication rates. Part of the lineage leading to Storm et al. (2010) meta-analysis.

papers_wanted.title: Commentary: False-Positive Effect in the Radin Double-Slit Experiment on Observer Consciousness as Determined With the Advanced Meta-Experimental Protocol
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct target of walleczek_2020_false_positive_radin. Radin et al.'s defense of the DS-experiment against the AMP-based critique; completing this exchange is needed for Controversy #4.

papers_wanted.title: The Unbearable Fear of Psi: On Scientific Suppression in the 21st Century
papers_wanted.why_needed: Directly addresses scientific suppression of psi research — cited by wahbeh_2018_exceptional, schooler_2018_entertaining, and other library papers. Documents specific instances of institutional resistance to anomalous cognition research. Fills a gap in the methodology/theory folder on sociological barriers to psi investigation.

papers_wanted.title: Discarnate Readings by Claimant Mediums: Assessing Phenomenology and Accuracy Under Beyond Double-Blind Conditions
papers_wanted.why_needed: Extends Beischel triple-blind mediumship protocol (beischel_2007_anomalous, beischel_2015_anomalous) to 'beyond double-blind' conditions. Directly cited by wahbeh_2018_mixed as evidence for veracity of mediumistic information. Fills gap in mediumship evidence base.

papers_wanted.title: Do Religious Mediumship Dissociative Experiences Conform to the Sociocognitive Theory of Dissociation?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational study on whether mediumship dissociation is pathological or normative in Brazilian Spiritist context. Cited by wahbeh_2018_mixed as key evidence that channelers are psychologically healthy. Central to the mediumship pathology debate (#6 controversy).

papers_wanted.title: Frontal Electroencephalographic (EEG) Activity and Mediumship: A Comparative Study Between Spiritist Mediums and Controls
papers_wanted.why_needed: One of very few EEG studies of trance channeling/mediumship. Found greater beta power in channelers vs. controls. Directly discussed in wahbeh_2018_mixed as preliminary physiological evidence. Fills gap in mediumship physiology literature.

papers_wanted.title: Hidden Communicative Competence: Case Study Evidence Using Eye-Tracking and Video Analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Jaswal et al. 2020; first case study using eye-tracking to show a nonspeaking autistic individual looked at letters before typing them, providing initial evidence against cueing. Essential historical anchor for Controversy #9 (facilitated communication).

papers_wanted.title: Systematic Review of Facilitated Communication 2014–2018 Finds No New Evidence That Messages Delivered Using Facilitated Communication Are Authored by the Person with Disability
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key skeptical systematic review concluding no evidence for authorship in facilitated communication. Directly challenged by Jaswal et al. 2020's eye-tracking findings. Essential Con-side paper for Controversy #9.

papers_wanted.title: Authorship in Facilitated Communication: An Analysis of 11 Cases
papers_wanted.why_needed: Analyzed 11 cases of facilitated communication and found assistant influence on text. Key skeptical empirical evidence cited by Jaswal et al. 2020 as the basis for the scientific dismissal they challenge. Important Con-side paper for Controversy #9.

papers_wanted.title: Possible PK Influence on the Resuscitation of Anesthetized Mice
papers_wanted.why_needed: Historical precursor to bio-PK and healing intention research. First to document 'linger effects' (space conditioning) in a laboratory setting -- the concept directly tested by radin_2004_possible. Key early evidence for biological effects of intention.

papers_wanted.title: Attention Focusing Facilitated Through Remote Mental Interaction
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: The original AFFE experiment that all 11 studies in Schmidt 2012's meta-analysis replicated. Foundational to the entire attention-focusing facilitation paradigm and the DMILS research tradition.

papers_wanted.title: Effectiveness of Distant Healing for Patients with Chronic Fatigue Syndrome: A Randomised Controlled Partially Blinded Trial (EUHEALS)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major European RCT of distant healing (null result) cited by Schmidt 2012. Fills a gap in the healing/distant intention evidence base alongside Astin 2000 and Benson 2006.

papers_wanted.title: Review of Consciousness-Related Double-Slit Experiments
papers_wanted.why_needed: Independent review of ~30 double-slit consciousness experiments across five labs, concluding evidence supports a CCI-like effect. Directly cited in radin_2025_observer (ref 30). Would fill a gap as the only independent systematic review of this research program.

papers_wanted.title: Consciousness-Related Interactions in a Double-Slit Optical System: Replication
papers_wanted.why_needed: Second independent replication of the Radin double-slit experiment, reporting significant results. One of only two independent replications cited in radin_2025_observer (ref 29). Important for Controversy #4 evidence base.

papers_wanted.title: Posttraumatic Stress Symptoms Following Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational study showing NDE experiencers are NOT more likely to develop PTSD despite more intrusive thoughts, and instead show more post-traumatic growth. Cited by martial_2025_nde_clinical_relevance for the claim that NDEs confer protective influence against PTSD.

papers_wanted.title: The Near-Death Experience Content (NDE-C) Scale: Development and Psychometric Validation
papers_wanted.why_needed: Validation of the NDE-C scale, the modern measurement instrument for NDEs. Cited by martial_2025_nde_clinical_relevance as the recommended tool for clinical NDE identification. Essential methodology paper for the library.

papers_wanted.title: A Close Look at Therapeutic Touch
papers_wanted.why_needed: Famous JAMA study where a 9-year-old (Emily Rosa) tested whether Therapeutic Touch practitioners could detect a human energy field — they could not (correct identification rate no better than chance). Key skeptical paper for the TT component of Controversy #5; cited by Astin et al. 2000 (ref 15).

papers_wanted.title: Wave-Packet Reduction as a Medium of Communication
papers_wanted.why_needed: Original experiment testing whether consciousness collapses the quantum wave-packet. Bierman (2003) is a direct conceptual replication correcting design flaws (delay too short, verbal reports as DV). Foundational to Controversy #4 (double-slit/quantum observer effects) and the consciousness-causes-collapse research program.

papers_wanted.title: Differential Event-Related Potentials to Targets and Decoys in a Guessing Task
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion to Don et al. (1998) ERP psi study; reports differential ERP responses in a guessing paradigm. Direct precursor to the Moulton & Kosslyn (2008) fMRI approach. Cited by moulton_2008_using.

papers_wanted.title: Statistical Evidence in Experimental Psychology: An Empirical Comparison Using 855 t Tests
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key methodology paper comparing Bayesian and frequentist evidence across 855 psychological tests. Cited by Rouder et al. (2013) to contextualize the strength of psi Bayes factors. Central to the meta-debate controversy (#10) on appropriate statistical methods in parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: Intercessory Prayer for the Alleviation of Ill Health
papers_wanted.why_needed: Gold-standard Cochrane systematic review of intercessory prayer RCTs. Found fewer deaths in prayer condition with significant results for high-risk/motivated subjects (P < .00001). Directly cited by Schlitz et al. 2012 and relevant to controversy #5 (distant healing/prayer).

papers_wanted.title: A Systematic Review of the Empirical Literature on Intercessory Prayer
papers_wanted.why_needed: Meta-analysis of 17 RCTs finding small but significant effects for intercessory prayer under both random and fixed effect models. Cited by Schlitz et al. 2012 and relevant to controversy #5 (distant healing/prayer). Would complement Masters 2007 and Roberts 2011 perspectives.

papers_wanted.title: The Feeling of Being Stared At — Experimental
papers_wanted.why_needed: First published experimental study on the feeling of being stared at (1913). Historical precursor to the entire SOBA research program. Cited by Radin (2005) and Sheldrake (2005).

papers_wanted.title: A Controlled Study of Psychometry Using Psychic and Non-Psychic Claimants with Actual and False Readings Using a Mixed-Methods Approach
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Most rigorous modern controlled experimental test of psychometry claims. Uses both psychic and non-psychic participants with actual vs. false readings. Directly cited by simmonds_moore_2024_psychometry; fills the gap of having no experimental psychometry studies in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Comparing a Free-Response Psychometry Test with a Free-Response Visual Imagery Test for a Non-Psychic Sample
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Pioneering modern experimental psychometry study with general population participants. First in a series of Parra & Argibay psychometry experiments (2007, 2008, 2009) that revived systematic psychometry research. Cited by simmonds_moore_2024_psychometry.

papers_wanted.title: The Psychology of Anomalous Experiences: A Rediscovery
papers_wanted.journal: Psychology of Consciousness: Theory, Research, and Practice
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational framework paper for the study of anomalous experiences, by three leading researchers. Likely cited by multiple library papers. Provides theoretical context for anomalous experience research across multiple psi domains in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Event-Related Correlations between Brain Electrical Activities of Separated Human Subjects: Preliminary Results of a Replication Study
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the 47th Annual Convention of the Parapsychological Association
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct replication of wackermann_2003_correlations (in catalog). Extends the EEG brain-to-brain correlation evidence chain. Cited in Table S1 of tressoldi_2016_brain_interaction.

papers_wanted.title: Generalised Quantum Theory - Basic Idea and General Intuition: A Background Story and Overview
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical framework (Generalized Quantum Theory) for non-local mind-matter and mind-mind correlations. Referenced by multiple telepathy and PK papers in the library as theoretical justification. Cited in tressoldi_2016_brain_interaction introduction.

papers_wanted.title: Correlated Cerebral Events between Physically and Sensory Isolated Pairs of Subjects Exposed to Yoked Circumcerebral Magnetic Fields
papers_wanted.why_needed: Peer-reviewed brain-to-brain EEG study using circumcerebral magnetic fields. Published in Neuroscience Letters, one of the few non-parapsychology journals publishing this type of work. Cited in Table S1 of tressoldi_2016_brain_interaction.

papers_wanted.title: Near-Death and Out-of-Body Experiences in the Blind: A Study of Apparent Eyeless Vision
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational NDE study in blind persons — one of the strongest evidence cases for veridical perception during NDEs. Received a perfect 10/10 quality rating in Hashemi et al. 2023 systematic review. Directly relevant to controversy #7 and provides unique evidence that NDE perceptions cannot be explained by prior visual experience.

papers_wanted.title: Psi Performance and Autonomic Nervous System Activity
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Braud's foundational paper on autonomic nervous system measures in psi research. Direct precursor to Braud & Schlitz 1991 (in catalog). Cited by Radin 1997 as establishing the largest body of physiological psi experiments. Anchors the distant mental influence research program.

papers_wanted.title: Gathering in More Sheep and Goats: A Meta-Analysis of Forced-Choice Sheep-Goat ESP Studies, 1994-2015
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Meta-analysis of the sheep-goat effect in forced-choice ESP; directly cited by Sheldrake et al. 2025 to explain why belief/attitude moderates telecommunication telepathy results. Fills a gap in the methodology/theory holdings.

papers_wanted.title: Death-Bed Visions: The Psychical Experiences of the Dying
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational text on deathbed visions that coined the systematic study of end-of-life apparitions. Directly cited by Greyson (2010) as providing 'one of the strongest arguments in favour of survival'. Fills a major historical gap in the NDE/survival section of the library. Precursor to Osis & Haraldsson (1977) and all modern deathbed vision research.

papers_wanted.title: Are There Demonstrable Effects of Distant Intercessory Prayer? A Meta-Analytic Review
papers_wanted.why_needed: The original 14-study meta-analysis of distant intercessory prayer that masters_2007_prayer updates. Direct precursor to the library's primary null-result meta-analysis for prayer. Would complete the extended-by chain and strengthen Controversy #5.

papers_wanted.title: Experiments on Distant Intercessory Prayer: God, Science, and the Lesson of Massah
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational methodological critique of distant intercessory prayer experiments, questioning theological assumptions and control-group validity. Cited by Masters et al. 2007 as key critique of the IP paradigm. Fills the methodological-critique gap in Controversy #5.

papers_wanted.title: Comparing Meta-Analyses and Preregistered Multiple-Laboratory Replication Projects
papers_wanted.why_needed: Demonstrates that meta-analysis effect size estimates are on average three times larger than preregistered replication estimates across 15 psychology domains. Directly relevant to Controversy #10 (meta-debate) and to evaluating whether psi meta-analyses overestimate effect sizes.

papers_wanted.title: How Smokers Change Their World and How the World Responds: Testing the Oscillatory Nature of Micro-Psychokinetic Observer Effects on Addiction-Related Stimuli
papers_wanted.why_needed: Develops the three temporal analysis methods (maximum BF, BF energy, FFT) used in the exploratory analyses of Maier et al. (2020). Extends the oscillation prediction from GQT/MPI to empirical testing. Part of the same research program as maier_2018_intentional_observer and maier_dechamps_2018_smoker_micro_pk.

papers_wanted.title: Finding and Correcting Flawed Research Literatures
papers_wanted.why_needed: Avowedly skeptical researchers who successfully replicated the ganzfeld ESP experiment, obtaining the same 32% hit rate as prior meta-analyses. Unique as a skeptical replication that confirmed the psi effect. Cited by Tressoldi et al. (2010) and relevant to Controversy #1 (ganzfeld telepathy).

papers_wanted.title: A Meta-Analysis with Nothing to Hide: Reply to Hyman (2010)
papers_wanted.why_needed: Completes the Storm–Hyman exchange in Psychological Bulletin. The library already holds Hyman (2010) critique (hyman_2010_meta_analysis_conceals) and the original Storm et al. (2010) meta-analysis (storm_2010_metaanalysis), but is missing this direct reply. Essential for balanced coverage of Controversy #1.

papers_wanted.title: Near-Death Experiences in Cardiac Arrest: Visions of a Dying Brain or Visions of a New Science of Consciousness
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key review article synthesizing the British prospective NDE study findings and arguing that NDE data challenge the assumption that consciousness is localized exclusively in the brain. Cited by van Lommel (2006) and forms part of the core trio of prospective NDE studies (Dutch, American, British) in controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: An Exploration of Sensory and Movement Differences from the Perspective of Individuals with Autism
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion empirical paper to Donnellan et al. (2013) in same journal issue; gathers first-person accounts from individuals with autism about sensory and movement differences. Cited by Donnellan paper. Would provide the empirical/experiential foundation supporting the theoretical framework. Fills gap: library has the theory paper but not the companion qualitative study.

papers_wanted.title: Anticipatory Alarm Behavior in Bengalese Finches
papers_wanted.why_needed: Animal presentiment study cited in Radin 2011 as evidence for retrocausation extending beyond humans. Novel experimental paradigm (bird alarm behavior to snake video). Important for completeness of presentiment literature review.

papers_wanted.title: Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioral Sciences
papers_wanted.why_needed: The foundational reference for statistical power analysis in behavioral sciences. Directly relevant to psi methodology debates: Gigerenzer (2004), Button et al. (2013), and Kennedy's methodology series all build on Cohen's power framework. Essential for understanding why low-powered psi experiments produce unreliable results and why failure to replicate is uninformative without power analysis.

papers_wanted.title: A New Instrument for Measuring the Sheep-Goat Variable: Its Psychometric Properties and Factor Structure
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational psychometric paper introducing the Australian Sheep-Goat Scale, the most widely used individual-difference measure in experimental psi research. Referenced by at least 6 library papers (Alcock 2003, Escola-Gascon 2023, Gray & Gallo 2016, Kennedy 1981, Parker 2003, Schwartz 2010). The sheep-goat effect (believers scoring higher than skeptics in psi tasks) is one of the most replicated findings in parapsychology. Fills a methodology gap.

papers_wanted.title: Project Deep Quest: A Prototype Experiment in the Application of Intuitively Derived Data in Marine Archaeology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to the Alexandria Project; established the consensual multi-viewer RV methodology used in schwartz_1980_eastern_harbor and schwartz_2019_location. Demonstrated RV could locate a previously unknown shipwreck at 92+ meters depth from up to 4,800 km away. Cited by schwartz_1980_eastern_harbor.

papers_wanted.title: Leveraging the Autism Puzzle: A Different Starting Point
papers_wanted.why_needed: Proposes 'presuming competence' framework for autism. Cited by jaswal_2019 as alternative to deficit model. Important for understanding movement/sensorimotor explanations.
papers_wanted.notes: Previously marked acquired in error. The file Donnellan_et_al_2013_Leveraging_Autism.pdf actually contains "Rethinking Autism" (donnellan_2013_rethinking), not this paper.

papers_wanted.title: Further Evidence of the Possibility of Exploiting Anticipatory Physiological Signals to Assist Implicit Intuition of Random Events
papers_wanted.why_needed: Second precursor to Tressoldi 2011 — extends the HR prediction paradigm. Part of the Tressoldi prediction-accuracy program that culminates in the pupil dilation study. JSE papers are generally accessible.
papers_wanted.notes: Previously marked acquired in error. The Tressoldi 2010 in the library is "Extrasensory Perception and Quantum Models" (tressoldi_2010_extrasensory), not this paper.

papers_wanted.title: Integrative Noetic Therapies as Adjuncts to Percutaneous Intervention During Unstable Coronary Syndromes: Monitoring and Actualization of Noetic Training (MANTRA) Feasibility Pilot
papers_wanted.why_needed: Constituent study of Roberts et al. 2009 Cochrane review (1 of 10 RCTs). Pilot trial of noetic therapies including intercessory prayer in CCU patients. Relevant to Controversy #5.

papers_wanted.title: The Efficacy of Prayer: A Triple-Blind Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Earliest included RCT in Roberts et al. 2009 Cochrane review. Triple-blind prayer study in childhood leukaemia (n=18). Historical landmark as one of the first experimental tests of intercessory prayer.

papers_wanted.title: Dream Telepathy: Experiments in Nocturnal ESP
papers_wanted.why_needed: Primary source for the entire Maimonides dream ESP research program. Central to Child (1985), which is a defense of these experiments. Contains the data summarized in Child's Table 1 and is the foundational work for dream telepathy research.

papers_wanted.title: Does Psi Exist? Replicable Evidence for an Anomalous Process of Information Transfer
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark autoganzfeld paper by Honorton (co-researcher in the Maimonides program defended by Child 1985) and Bem. Foundational to the ganzfeld controversy (#1) and cited by virtually every ganzfeld paper in the library. Direct continuation of the research program Child defended.

papers_wanted.title: ESP and Parapsychology: A Critical Re-evaluation
papers_wanted.why_needed: Primary skeptical critique of the Maimonides dream experiments, extensively analyzed and rebutted in Child (1985). Important skeptical counterpoint in the meta-debate controversy (#10). Child demonstrated that Hansel exaggerated sensory cuing opportunities and implicitly relied on fraud hypothesis.

papers_wanted.title: Do You Know Who Is Calling? Experiments on Anomalous Cognition in Phone Call Receivers
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key null-result telephone telepathy study (26.7% vs 25% chance, non-significant). Only videotaped lab-based replication attempt. Essential skeptical counterpoint to Sheldrake's positive findings and cited by Wahbeh et al. 2024.

papers_wanted.title: Do You Know Who Is on the Phone? Replication of an Experiment on Telephone Telepathy
papers_wanted.why_needed: Conference paper reporting another non-significant telephone telepathy replication. Paired with Schmidt 2009 as the main independent failure to replicate. Cited by Wahbeh 2024 and important for balanced assessment of telephone telepathy evidence.

papers_wanted.title: Genetics of Psychic Ability: A Pilot Case-Control Exome Sequencing Study
papers_wanted.why_needed: Preliminary genetic evidence for psi abilities using exome sequencing. Directly relevant to the genetic relatedness findings in Wahbeh 2024 telephone telepathy study. Part of the IONS research program examining biological correlates of psi.

papers_wanted.title: Psychic Pets: A Survey in North-West England
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct companion study to brown_sheldrake_1998_perceptive_pets — the Ramsbottom, Greater Manchester survey whose results are cross-culturally compared in the California paper. Essential for Controversy #12 (animal telepathy).

papers_wanted.title: Perceptive Pets: A Survey in London
papers_wanted.why_needed: Third data point in Sheldrake's cross-cultural pet survey series (London), extending the Ramsbottom and California surveys. Completes the comparative picture for Controversy #12.

papers_wanted.title: The Present Outlook on the Question of Psi in Animals
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational early assessment of animal psi evidence from the Rhine lab era. Historical precursor to the modern animal telepathy research program. Cited by Brown & Sheldrake (1998).

papers_wanted.title: Searching for the Impossible: Parapsychology's Elusive Quest
papers_wanted.why_needed: High-profile skeptical argument in a top-tier psychology journal that psi is impossible in principle. Cited by Sheldrake & Stedall (2024) and relevant to Controversy #10 (meta-debate). Represents the strongest a priori rejection position in the psi debate.

papers_wanted.title: A Bayes Factor Meta-Analysis of Bem's ESP Claim
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key Bayesian reanalysis of Bem's 2011 Feeling the Future data; directly cited by kekecs_2023_transparent_psi and multiple library papers in Controversy #2; provides the Bayesian framework that influenced the TPP design

papers_wanted.title: Fearing the Future of Empirical Psychology: Bem's (2011) Evidence of Psi as a Case Study of Deficiencies in Modal Research Practice
papers_wanted.why_needed: Identifies specific methodological deficiencies in Bem's 2011 paradigm that the Transparent Psi Project was explicitly designed to address; cited as ref [60] in kekecs_2023_transparent_psi as the key reference for 'deficiencies in modal research practice'

papers_wanted.title: Testing for Questionable Research Practices in a Meta-Analysis: An Example from Experimental Parapsychology
papers_wanted.why_needed: Tests for questionable research practices in the Bem FTF meta-analytic database; directly relevant to the 'pure bias theory' tested by kekecs_2023_transparent_psi; fills gap in Controversy #2 evidence base

papers_wanted.title: Enhancing Human Performance: Issues, Theories, and Techniques
papers_wanted.why_needed: The 1988 National Research Council report that preceded the AIR/Star Gate evaluation. It reviewed remote viewing research through 1986 and found 'little or no support' for remote viewing, but its methodological criticisms shaped the improved SAIC experiments reviewed in the Mumford et al. 1995 AIR report. Essential predecessor document for the remote viewing controversy.

papers_wanted.title: Multiple Channels in Precognitive Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Preliminary report of Dunne & Bisaha precognitive remote viewing work published in Science (Vol. 201, p. 431), one of the few psi studies to appear in a top-tier journal. Cited by dunne_bisaha_1979_precognitive_remote and contextualizes the Chicago RV replication series.

papers_wanted.title: Psychophysical Interaction with a Double-Slit Interference Pattern
papers_wanted.why_needed: Internet-based double-slit experiment (2013). Direct successor to radin_2012_consciousness and predecessor to radin_2016_double_slit. Previously acquired PDF was wrong (contained the 2012 paper instead). Need to re-acquire the correct PDF.
sources_tried: Previous download attempt yielded wrong PDF (2012 paper instead of 2013)

papers_wanted.title: Terminal lucidity in patients with chronic schizophrenia and dementia: a survey of the literature
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to nahm_2012_terminal_lucidity; first paper to coin 'terminal lucidity' as a research term; foundational to the entire research program on this phenomenon

papers_wanted.title: At the Hour of Death
papers_wanted.why_needed: Classic foundational work on deathbed observations; cited by nahm_2012_terminal_lucidity and relevant to the NDE/survival controversy; Haraldsson is co-author of the terminal lucidity paper

papers_wanted.title: Surges of electroencephalographic activity at the time of death: a case series
papers_wanted.why_needed: Documents unexplained EEG surges near death; cited by nahm_2012_terminal_lucidity; direct precursor to borjigin_2013_surge_dying already in catalog; key evidence in the NDE neurophysiology debate

papers_wanted.title: Comfort for the dying: five year retrospective and one year prospective studies of end of life experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Prospective and retrospective study of end-of-life experiences including terminal lucidity; cited by nahm_2012_terminal_lucidity; published in same journal; provides empirical prevalence data

papers_wanted.title: Near-death experience in survivors of cardiac arrest: a prospective study in the Netherlands
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational prospective NDE study cited by Borjigin 2013 and many other library papers; the largest prospective study of NDEs in cardiac arrest survivors; essential for Controversy #7

papers_wanted.title: Processed electroencephalogram during donation after cardiac death
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key companion paper cited by Borjigin 2013 — reports EEG surges (bispectral index) in humans undergoing organ donation after cardiac death; the only direct human parallel to Borjigin's rat findings

papers_wanted.title: 'There is nothing paranormal about near-death experiences' revisited: Comment on Mobbs and Watt
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct reply to Mobbs & Watt (2011) already in library; cited by Borjigin 2013; completes the debate pair for Controversy #7

papers_wanted.title: Distressing Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational classification paper for distressing NDEs (inverse/void/hellish taxonomy). Cassol et al. 2019 is a direct replication. Cited by multiple NDE papers in the library. Essential for Controversy #7 (NDEs/survival).

papers_wanted.title: Near-death experience in survivors of cardiac arrest: a prospective study in the Netherlands
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark prospective NDE study in The Lancet (N=344 cardiac arrest survivors, 18% reported NDE). One of the most cited NDE papers. Cited by Cassol 2019, Parnia 2014, and many other library papers. Essential for Controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: The Phenomenology of Near-Death Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Early foundational characterization of NDE phenomenology published in Am J Psychiatry. Historical precursor to the Greyson NDE Scale (1983) and distressing NDE taxonomy (1992). Cited by Cassol 2019.

papers_wanted.title: Depersonalization in the Face of Life-Threatening Danger: A Description
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational paper on depersonalization as a response to life-threatening danger, a key precursor to modern NDE research. Cited by multiple library NDE papers including Greyson 2001 and Greyson 1983 NDE Scale. Establishes the dissociation-danger link that NDE researchers built upon.

papers_wanted.title: Can Experiences Near Death Furnish Evidence of Life After Death?
papers_wanted.journal: Omega: Journal of Death and Dying
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion paper to Kelly 2001 where Kelly, Greyson, and Stevenson identify three NDE features (OBE, enhanced cognition, paranormal cognition) as evidence for survival. Directly cited by kelly_2001_nde_meeting_deceased as establishing the convergence-of-features framework. Part of the UVA NDE research program.

papers_wanted.title: Are Persons Reporting 'Near-Death Experiences' Really Near Death? A Study of Medical Records
papers_wanted.journal: Omega: Journal of Death and Dying
papers_wanted.why_needed: Established the medical-record closeness-to-death rating methodology used in Kelly 2001 and other UVA NDE studies. Found 45% of NDE reporters were truly near death. Foundational methodological paper for the entire UVA NDE research program.

papers_wanted.title: Near-death experience as a probe to explore (disconnected) consciousness
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major theoretical review by the same Coma Science Group positioning NDEs as a window into disconnected consciousness. Cited by the NDE-C scale paper (martial_2020_nde_c_scale). Published in a high-impact journal (TiCS), making it a key reference for the NDE consciousness survival controversy (#7).

papers_wanted.title: Incidence and correlates of near-death experiences in a cardiac care unit
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key prospective NDE study reporting that 10% of cardiac arrest survivors have NDEs. Foundational reference for NDE incidence rates, cited by martial_2020_nde_c_scale and multiple other NDE papers. Fills gap in prospective NDE studies alongside van Lommel 2001 and Parnia AWARE studies.

papers_wanted.title: Prevalence of near-death experiences in people with and without REM sleep intrusion
papers_wanted.why_needed: Large-scale epidemiological study linking NDE prevalence to REM sleep intrusion, extending Nelson et al. 2006 (nelson_2006_rem_nde) hypothesis. Cited by martial_2020_nde_c_scale. Relevant to NDE consciousness survival controversy (#7) from the neurophysiological explanation side.

papers_wanted.title: Recollections of Death: A Medical Investigation
papers_wanted.why_needed: First systematic medical investigation of veridical NDE perception; Ring & Cooper 1997 directly cite Sabom's work on apparently impossible visual perception during NDEs as key predecessor; Blackmore 1993 specifically critiqued Sabom's findings.

papers_wanted.title: Out-of-Body Experiences in the Blind
papers_wanted.why_needed: Only prior systematic survey of OBEs in blind persons (N=21 in Australia); Ring & Cooper 1997 directly extended this study, noting Irwin found no congenitally blind OBEr. Essential predecessor for understanding the Ring & Cooper contribution.

papers_wanted.title: Near-Death Experience in Survivors of Cardiac Arrest: A Prospective Study in the Netherlands
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark prospective study of NDEs in 344 cardiac arrest survivors; foundational for the NDE evidence base and Controversy #7. Complements Ring & Cooper 1997's qualitative findings with controlled prospective methodology.

papers_wanted.title: Out-of-body experiences in the blind
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Ring & Cooper 1997; the only prior systematic attempt to survey OBEs in blind persons. Irwin found 3 OBErs among 21 blind respondents but none congenitally blind — Ring & Cooper explicitly set out to extend this study. Essential for the NDE/OBE-in-blind research lineage.

papers_wanted.title: Heading toward Omega: In Search of the Meaning of the Near-Death Experience
papers_wanted.why_needed: Ring's second major NDE book, establishing the transformative-aftereffects research paradigm. Cited by Ring & Cooper 1997 for the case of a nearsighted woman with detailed veridical perception during NDE (including dust on light fixtures). Key foundational text in NDE research.

papers_wanted.title: Out-of-the-Body Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational systematic survey of OBEs, cited by Ring & Cooper 1997 for evidence of transcendental awareness including 'crystal clear' perception, seeing through obstacles, and synesthetic experiences. Early empirical OBE research predating modern NDE studies.

papers_wanted.title: Surge of neurophysiological activity in the dying human brain
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Reports surge of gamma oscillations in two dying human patients, extending Borjigin et al. 2013 rat findings to humans. Directly relevant to AWARE II EEG findings and the debate about whether cortical activity during cardiac arrest reflects consciousness. Central to Controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Markers of consciousness in the EEG of cardiac arrest patients during CPR
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion analysis to AWARE II providing detailed EEG methodology and biomarker analysis for consciousness detection during cardiac arrest. Extends the electrocortical findings reported in the main AWARE II paper.

papers_wanted.title: After: A Doctor Explores What Near-Death Experiences Reveal about Life and Beyond
papers_wanted.why_needed: Comprehensive overview of NDE research by the creator of the Greyson NDE Scale, synthesizing decades of evidence. Greyson is the most prolific NDE researcher in the library (7+ papers) and this book provides his mature theoretical framework. Fills a gap as a book-length treatment by a key figure.

papers_wanted.title: Psycho-Physical Interactions with a Single-Photon Double-Slit Optical System
papers_wanted.why_needed: Third in the Radin double-slit experiment series (after 2012 and 2013), directly critiqued by Walleczek & von Stillfried (2019). Completes the set of DS experiments whose validity is at stake in controversy #4.

papers_wanted.title: Anomalous Experiences, Psi and Functional Neuroimaging
papers_wanted.why_needed: Methodological critique of psi neuroimaging studies; cited by both Tressoldi 2014 BBI study and Achterberg 2005 distant healing study. Fills the skeptical/methodological gap for evaluating EEG/fMRI-based brain-to-brain interaction claims.

papers_wanted.title: The Psychology of Paranormal Belief: A Researcher's Handbook
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational reference book for the psychology of paranormal belief, cited by multiple library papers on belief correlates (Dagnall et al. 2025, Drinkwater et al. 2022, Gray & Gallo 2016). Provides the theoretical framework underpinning library papers on cognitive styles and paranormal belief.

papers_wanted.title: An exploratory study of the effects of paranormal and spiritual experiences on peoples' lives and well-being
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational study linking paranormal experiences to enhanced wellbeing (connectedness, happiness, meaning). Directly cited by Dagnall et al. 2025 as key evidence for adaptive function of paranormal belief. Fills a gap in the library's coverage of paranormal experience and psychological outcomes.

papers_wanted.title: Psilocybin can occasion mystical-type experiences having substantial and sustained personal meaning and spiritual significance
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational psilocybin mystical-experience study; cited by Carhart-Harris 2012 and Barrett & Griffiths 2018 in the library; establishes the phenomenological basis for psychedelic consciousness research relevant to filter theories of consciousness.

papers_wanted.title: Positron emission tomography and fluorodeoxyglucose studies of metabolic hyperfrontality and psychopathology in the psilocybin model of psychosis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key PET predecessor to Carhart-Harris 2012; found increased glucose metabolism under psilocybin — the opposite of the fMRI decreases found in 2012. The discrepancy between these two studies is a central interpretive puzzle in psychedelic neuroscience.

papers_wanted.title: The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational theoretical framework invoked by Carhart-Harris 2012 to explain psychedelic state; the free-energy principle is central to the 'reducing valve' interpretation of consciousness that connects psychedelic research to filter theories relevant to psi debates.

papers_wanted.title: Differences in Cognitive-Perceptual Factors Arising From Variations in Self-Professed Paranormal Ability
papers_wanted.why_needed: Quantitative companion to Drinkwater 2022 qualitative study already in library. Examines cognitive-perceptual factors in self-professed paranormal ability using N=917, providing the large-sample quantitative context for the same research program's qualitative findings.

papers_wanted.title: Understanding the Unknown: A Thematic Analysis of Subjective Paranormal Experiences
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Drinkwater 2022 already in library. First qualitative study of subjective paranormal experiences from the Drinkwater-Dagnall program; the 2022 paper explicitly builds on and extends this work's thematic framework.

papers_wanted.title: Say from Whence You Owe This Strange Intelligence: Investigating Explanatory Systems of Spiritualist Mental Mediumship Using Interpretative Phenomenological Analysis
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key prior qualitative study of spiritualist mediums using IPA, directly cited by Drinkwater 2022 as primary precedent for qualitative investigation of paranormal abilities. Would strengthen the library's qualitative mediumship coverage alongside Beischel's quantitative work.

papers_wanted.title: From the Phenomenology to the Mechanisms of Consciousness: Integrated Information Theory 3.0
papers_wanted.why_needed: The formal mathematical specification of IIT 3.0, the most detailed formulation of the theory reviewed in Tononi & Koch 2015. Essential companion paper for understanding the Phi_max calculus and postulate formalization.

papers_wanted.title: An information integration theory of consciousness
papers_wanted.why_needed: The original IIT paper introducing the concept of integrated information as the basis for consciousness. Foundational to the entire IIT research program; cited by Tononi & Koch 2015 and the companion IIT 3.0 formalism paper already in the library.

papers_wanted.title: A theoretically based index of consciousness independent of sensory processing and behavior
papers_wanted.why_needed: Introduces the perturbational complexity index (PCI), the primary empirical measure inspired by IIT. PCI reliably distinguishes conscious from unconscious states and is the strongest empirical validation of IIT's predictions. Cited by Tononi & Koch 2015 as key evidence.

papers_wanted.title: The Importance of Quantum Decoherence in Brain Processes
papers_wanted.why_needed: The most prominent critique of quantum consciousness proposals. Tegmark calculated decoherence times of 10^-13 s for microtubules, arguing quantum effects are far too brief to be biologically relevant. Directly critiques the Orch OR theory (hameroff_penrose_2014_orch_or) and is cited extensively in the quantum consciousness debate.

papers_wanted.title: Quantum Mechanics in the Brain
papers_wanted.why_needed: Published in Nature, this challenges Orch OR with a thought experiment about binocular rivalry and Schrodinger's cat, questioning where in the brain objective reduction would occur. Key skeptical voice from a leading consciousness neuroscientist.

papers_wanted.title: Penrose-Hameroff Orchestrated Objective-Reduction Proposal for Human Consciousness Is Not Biologically Feasible
papers_wanted.why_needed: Detailed biophysics critique arguing Orch OR's proposed tubulin switching mechanism via London forces is not feasible, and that GTP hydrolysis would be prohibitively expensive. Directly critiques hameroff_penrose_2014_orch_or; the Orch OR paper responds to this critique in Section 8.

papers_wanted.title: Replications in Psychology Research: How Often Do They Really Occur?
papers_wanted.why_needed: Provides the key statistic that only 1.07% of psychology papers are replications, with only 18% being direct replications — foundational data for the replication crisis debate. Cited by Ioannidis 2012 and central to the meta-debate controversy (#10).

papers_wanted.title: The Persistence of Underpowered Studies in Psychological Research: Causes, Consequences, and Remedies
papers_wanted.why_needed: Source of the widely cited finding that average statistical power in psychology is ~35%. This figure is central to false-positive rate calculations in Ioannidis 2012 and Pashler & Harris 2012, and directly relevant to power debates in parapsychology.

papers_wanted.title: Lessons From the First Two Years of Operating a Study Registry
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Watt & Kennedy 2017 prospective meta-analysis proposal; describes lessons learned from operating the KPU Study Registry, which is the infrastructure underlying the ganzfeld exemplar. Fills a gap in the library's methodology collection on study registration practices.

papers_wanted.title: Research Note: Replicable Evidence of Psi: A Revision of Milton's (1999) Meta-Analysis of Ganzfeld Databases
papers_wanted.why_needed: Key node in the ganzfeld debate chain: Storm's revision of Milton 1999 that argued for replicable psi evidence. Cited by Watt & Kennedy 2017, Storm et al. 2010, and relevant to the ganzfeld controversy (#1). Fills a gap between Milton 1999 and Storm 2001/2010 in the library.

papers_wanted.title: Psilocybin Occasioned Mystical-Type Experiences: Immediate and Persisting Dose-Related Effects
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark double-blind placebo-controlled dose-response study showing psilocybin produces complete mystical experiences in 61-67% of participants at high doses. Central to the empirical evidence reviewed in Barrett & Griffiths 2018. Foundational for the psychedelic therapy literature.

papers_wanted.title: Psilocybin Produces Substantial and Sustained Decreases in Depression and Anxiety in Patients with Life-Threatening Cancer: A Randomized Double-Blind Trial
papers_wanted.why_needed: Major RCT demonstrating that mystical experience during psilocybin sessions mediates therapeutic outcomes for cancer-related distress. Key evidence for the mediating role of mystical experience cited in Barrett & Griffiths 2018.
