papers_wanted.title: Drugs and Mysticism: An Analysis of the Relationship Between Psychedelic Drugs and the Mystical Consciousness
papers_wanted.why_needed: The original Good Friday experiment — the first controlled study of hallucinogen-occasioned mystical experience. Historical foundation for the entire line of research reviewed in Barrett & Griffiths 2018. Replicated and extended by Griffiths et al. 2006.

papers_wanted.title: Complementarity is a Useful Concept for Consciousness Studies. A Reminder
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct precursor to Weak Quantum Theory (WQT) by two of its three authors. Introduces the complementarity framework for consciousness that WQT formalizes axiomatically. Cited by atmanspacher_2002_weak_quantum and likely by walach_2014_entanglement and walach_2020_inner_experience.

papers_wanted.title: Pragmatic Information as a Non-Classical Concept to Describe Cognitive Systems
papers_wanted.why_needed: Introduces the concept of pragmatic information (complementarity of confirmation and novelty) that becomes central to von Lucadou's Model of Pragmatic Information (MPI) and the GQT program. Cited by atmanspacher_2002_weak_quantum; foundational to lucadou_2007_entanglement.

papers_wanted.title: Naturerklärung und Psyche
papers_wanted.why_needed: The foundational text for psychophysical parallelism and synchronicity that the entire GQT research program (Atmanspacher, Walach, von Lucadou) formalizes. Contains Jung's essay on synchronicity and Pauli's essay on Kepler, which together establish the conceptual basis for mind-matter complementarity. Cited by atmanspacher_2002_weak_quantum.
sources_not_yet_tried: English translation: The Interpretation of Nature and the Psyche (1955)

papers_wanted.title: Why Most Published Research Findings Are False
papers_wanted.why_needed: The single most-cited paper on false-positive rates in published science; foundational to the meta-debate controversy (#10); cited by Nosek et al. 2013, Simmons et al. 2011, Fanelli 2009, and multiple library methodology papers. Provides the theoretical framework for understanding why psi findings may not replicate.

papers_wanted.title: Accuracy and Replicability of Anomalous After-Death Communication Across Highly Skilled Mediums
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Primary pro-mediumship study that O'Keeffe & Wiseman 2005 was designed to address. Reported significant accuracy in mediumistic readings; central to the mediumship controversy (#6). Without this paper, the library lacks the key positive finding against which the O'Keeffe null result is compared.

papers_wanted.title: Accuracy and Replicability of Anomalous After-Death Communication Across Highly Skilled Mediums: A Critique
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct methodological critique of Schwartz et al. 2001 by the same authors as okeefe_2005_testing_mediumship. Forms a critique-response pair essential for understanding the mediumship controversy (#6).

papers_wanted.title: An Overview of Quantitatively Evaluated Studies with Mediums and Psychics
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of the American Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Most comprehensive quantitative review of mediumship studies at the time, cited extensively by O'Keeffe & Wiseman 2005 and foundational for the mediumship controversy (#6). Library currently lacks any historical mediumship review.

papers_wanted.title: Dose-Response Study of N,N-Dimethyltryptamine in Humans. I. Neuroendocrine, Autonomic, and Cardiovascular Effects
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational human DMT dose-response study establishing psychoactive plasma concentrations (~60 ng/mL for breakthrough). Key evidence used by Nichols 2017 to demonstrate pineal DMT production is insufficient by ~1000-fold. Essential for NDE/consciousness debate.

papers_wanted.title: The Hallucinogen N,N-Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) Is an Endogenous Sigma-1 Receptor Regulator
papers_wanted.why_needed: Published in Science; established DMT as endogenous sigma-1 receptor ligand (KD = 14.75 µM). Key pro-DMT evidence that Nichols 2017 critiques as insufficient for endogenous activation. Central to the DMT-consciousness debate.

papers_wanted.title: LC/MS/MS Analysis of the Endogenous Dimethyltryptamine Hallucinogens, Their Precursors, and Major Metabolites in Rat Pineal Gland Microdialysate
papers_wanted.why_needed: First definitive detection of DMT in rat pineal gland microdialysate via LC/MS/MS. Key empirical evidence cited by both proponents and skeptics of endogenous DMT hypothesis. Cited by Nichols 2017.

papers_wanted.title: Asphyxia-Activated Corticocardiac Signaling Accelerates Onset of Cardiac Arrest
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
papers_wanted.why_needed: From Borjigin lab; documents massive neurotransmitter surges during asphyxia (norepinephrine >30-fold, serotonin >20-fold, dopamine >12-fold). Cited by Nichols 2017 as alternative explanation for NDE altered states. Complements borjigin_2013_surge_dying already in library.

papers_wanted.title: Out-of-Body Experiences and Arousal
papers_wanted.why_needed: Companion to Nelson et al. 2006 REM intrusion hypothesis (nelson_2006_rem_nde on wishlist). Extends the arousal system model to OBEs, finding sleep-transition OBEs significantly more common in NDErs. Directly critiqued by Long & Holden 2007 (long_holden_2007_rem_nde_response). Relevant to controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Do Prevailing Societal Models Influence Reports of Near-Death Experiences? A Comparison of Accounts Reported Before and After 1975
papers_wanted.journal: Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease
papers_wanted.why_needed: Tests whether NDE reports changed after Moody's 1975 'Life After Life' made NDEs publicly known. Found tunnel reports increased post-1975 but 14 other NDE elements remained stable, suggesting NDEs are not culturally constructed. Cited by Long & Holden 2007 (long_holden_2007_rem_nde_response). Relevant to controversy #7.

papers_wanted.title: Science and the Supernatural
papers_wanted.why_needed: Landmark skeptical critique published in Science (Aug 26, 1955) that accused parapsychologists of fraud. First major skeptical paper in a prestige journal attacking ESP evidence. Central to Hyman's 'False Dichotomy' framework. Cited by hyman_1986_parapsychological_tutorial, and foundational to the meta-debate tracked in Controversy #10.

papers_wanted.title: Moving On: Autism and Movement Disturbance
papers_wanted.why_needed: Foundational journal article establishing the sensory-movement differences framework for autism. Directly cited by both robledo_2012_sensory_movement and donnellan_2013_rethinking as a theoretical foundation. The core argument — that movement disturbance can profoundly affect communication and social behavior, and that behaviors may not be what they seem — underpins the library's entire autism/nonverbal section.

papers_wanted.title: Report on the Census of Hallucinations
papers_wanted.journal: Proceedings of the Society for Psychical Research
papers_wanted.why_needed: Direct follow-up to Phantasms of the Living, conducting the first large-scale census (17,000 respondents) to establish base rates of hallucination in the general population. Confirmed that death-coincident hallucinations occurred at rates far exceeding chance, providing the statistical foundation that Phantasms lacked. Foundational to the evidential case for crisis telepathy.
